_meta.description: Apolytikia (troparia) and kontakia for movable feasts and major fixed-cycle saints. Same dual-key shape as lectionary: 'movable' keyed by pdist, 'fixed' keyed by canonical Menaion MM-DD.
_meta.license: Texts are original English translations of public-domain Greek/Slavonic hymnody. Theosis owns these English renderings.
_meta.coverage: Major movable feasts (Palm Sunday, Pascha, Ascension, Pentecost, All Saints) plus principal May fixed feasts. Long tail of saints/feasts populated incrementally.

0.title: Paschal Troparion
0.text: Christ is risen from the dead, trampling down death by death, and to those in the tombs bestowing life.

0.title: Paschal Kontakion
0.text: Though Thou didst descend into the grave, O Immortal One, yet didst Thou destroy the power of Hades, and didst rise as a victor, O Christ our God; Thou didst greet the myrrh-bearing women saying, Rejoice! and grant peace unto Thine apostles, Thou who givest resurrection to the fallen.

7.title: Troparion of Thomas Sunday
7.text: While the tomb was sealed, Thou, O Life, didst shine forth from the grave, O Christ God; and when the doors were shut, Thou didst come unto Thy disciples, the Resurrection of all, renewing through them an upright Spirit in us, according to Thy great mercy.

7.title: Kontakion of Thomas Sunday
7.text: With his searching right hand Thomas did probe Thy life-bestowing side, O Christ God; for when Thou didst enter while the doors were shut, he cried out unto Thee with the rest of the Apostles: Thou art my Lord and my God.

21.title: Kontakion of the Paralytic
21.text: By Thy divine intervention, O Lord, raise up my soul which is grievously paralyzed by sins of every kind and by unseemly deeds, even as Thou didst of old raise up the paralytic, that being saved I may cry unto Thee: Glory to Thy power, O compassionate Christ.

21.title: Troparion of the Paralytic
21.text: By the wondrous power of Thy divinity, O Lord, even as Thou didst raise up the paralytic of old, do Thou also raise me up, who am paralyzed with manifold sins of every kind and with unseemly deeds; that being saved I may cry unto Thee: Glory to Thy power, O compassionate Christ.

24.title: Troparion of Mid-Pentecost
24.text: At the midpoint of the feast, O Saviour, water my thirsty soul with the streams of true piety, for to all Thou didst cry out: Whosoever thirsteth, let him come to Me and drink. O Christ God, the Wellspring of life, glory to Thee.

24.title: Kontakion of Mid-Pentecost
24.text: At the mid-feast of the Law, O Christ God and Creator and Master of all things, Thou didst say unto them that stood by: Come and draw from the Water of Immortality. Wherefore we fall down before Thee and faithfully cry out: Grant unto us Thy compassions, O Master, for Thou art truly the Wellspring of life.

28.title: Kontakion of the Samaritan Woman
28.text: The Samaritan woman, having come in faith unto the well, beheld Thee, the Water of Wisdom. Drinking thereof abundantly, she inherited the Kingdom on high; therefore, she is forever exalted.

28.title: Troparion of the Samaritan Woman
28.text: When Thou drewest near the well, O Source of Mercy, the Samaritan woman besought Thy compassion, saying: Give me the Water of Faith, that I may receive the Font of Baptism, and rejoicing forever may sing the praises of Thy power.

39.title: Troparion of the Ascension
39.text: Thou hast ascended in glory, O Christ our God, granting joy to Thy disciples by the promise of the Holy Spirit. Through the blessing they were assured that Thou art the Son of God, the Redeemer of the world.

39.title: Kontakion of the Ascension
39.text: When Thou hadst fulfilled the dispensation for our sake, and united things on earth with the things in heaven, Thou didst ascend in glory, O Christ our God, in no wise departing from us, but remaining inseparable, and crying unto those who love Thee: I am with you, and there is none against you.

49.title: Troparion of Pentecost
49.text: Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, who hast made the fishermen wise, by sending down upon them the Holy Spirit, and through them drawing the world into Thy net. O Lover of mankind, glory to Thee.

49.title: Kontakion of Pentecost
49.text: When the Most High came down and confused the tongues, He divided the nations; but when He distributed the tongues of fire, He called all to unity. With one accord, therefore, let us glorify the all-Holy Spirit.

56.title: Troparion of All Saints
56.text: Adorned in the blood of Thy martyrs throughout all the world as in purple and fine linen, Thy Church, through them, doth cry unto Thee, O Christ God: send down Thy compassion upon Thy people; grant peace to Thy commonwealth, and to our souls great mercy.

56.title: Kontakion of All Saints
56.text: To Thee, O Lord, the Author of creation, the world doth offer the God-bearing martyrs as first-fruits of nature. By their entreaties keep Thy Church, Thine own commonwealth, in deep peace, through the Theotokos, O Thou greatly merciful.

-7.title: Troparion of Palm Sunday
-7.text: Confirming the universal Resurrection before Thy Passion, Thou didst raise Lazarus from the dead, O Christ our God. Therefore, like the children, we also bear emblems of victory and cry to Thee, the Conqueror of death: Hosanna in the highest, blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord.

-7.title: Kontakion of Palm Sunday
-7.text: Borne upon a throne in heaven, and upon a foal upon the earth, O Christ our God, Thou didst accept the praise of angels and the hymn of children crying out: Blessed art Thou that comest to call back Adam.

-2.title: Troparion of Holy Friday
-2.text: The noble Joseph, taking Thy most pure Body down from the Tree, wrapped it in clean linen with sweet spices and laid it in a new tomb. But on the third day Thou didst rise, O Lord, granting the world great mercy.

-2.title: Kontakion of Holy Friday
-2.text: He who clothed Himself in light as in a garment stood naked at the judgment seat, and on the cheek He received a blow from the hands which He Himself had formed. The lawless people nailed the Lord of glory to the Cross. Then the veil of the temple was rent in twain, the sun darkened, unable to look upon such outrage offered the Lord, before whom all creation trembleth.

-70.title: Troparion of the Publican and Pharisee
-70.text: Let us flee from the boasting of the Pharisee, O faithful, and learn humility from the publican's groaning. Let us cry out unto the Saviour: Be merciful unto us, O Thou who alone art ready to forgive.

-70.title: Kontakion of the Publican and Pharisee
-70.text: Let us flee the proud-tongued boasting of the Pharisee, and learn through tears the publican's broken sigh that ascended unto God. Wherefore let us cry out: O Saviour of the world, cleanse Thy servants and have mercy upon us.

-63.title: Troparion of the Prodigal Son
-63.text: Open unto me, O Giver of Life, the gates of repentance; for at dawn my spirit seeketh Thy holy temple, bearing a body all defiled. But in Thy compassion cleanse it by Thy loving mercy.

-63.title: Kontakion of the Prodigal Son
-63.text: Foolishly I have departed from Thy fatherly glory, and have squandered upon evils the wealth Thou gavest me. Wherefore I cry unto Thee with the voice of the Prodigal: I have sinned before Thee, O compassionate Father. Receive me, repentant, and make me as one of Thy hired servants.

-56.title: Troparion of Meatfare Sunday
-56.text: When Thou comest, O God, to earth with glory, all things shall tremble, and the river of fire shall flow before Thy judgment seat; the books shall be opened and hidden things made manifest. Then deliver me from that unquenchable fire, and count me worthy to stand at Thy right hand, O most righteous Judge.

-56.title: Kontakion of the Last Judgment
-56.text: When Thou comest, O God, to earth with glory, and all things tremble, and a river of fire floweth before Thy judgment seat, and the books are opened and the secrets of men are made manifest: then deliver me from that unquenchable fire, and count me worthy to stand on Thy right hand, O most righteous Judge.

-49.title: Troparion of Cheesefare Sunday
-49.text: The Lord, my Creator, took me as dust from the earth and formed me into a living creature, breathing into me the breath of life and honouring me as ruler upon earth over all things visible. Yet by the envy of the serpent I was driven from Paradise. Wherefore I cry: Receive me again, O Lord who lovest mankind.

-49.title: Kontakion of Forgiveness Sunday
-49.text: O Master, Guide of wisdom, Giver of prudent counsel, Instructor of the foolish and Champion of the poor: strengthen and enlighten my heart. Grant me the word, O Word of the Father, for behold, I shall not refrain my lips from crying out unto Thee: O Merciful One, have mercy upon me who am fallen.

-42.title: Troparion of the Sunday of Orthodoxy
-42.text: We venerate Thine immaculate icon, O Good One, asking forgiveness of our offences, O Christ our God; for of Thine own will Thou wast well-pleased to ascend the Cross in the flesh, that Thou mightest deliver from bondage to the enemy those whom Thou hast fashioned. Wherefore we cry unto Thee in thanksgiving: Thou hast filled all things with joy, O our Saviour, who didst come to save the world.

-42.title: Kontakion of the Sunday of Orthodoxy
-42.text: The undepictable Word of the Father became depictable when, O Theotokos, He took flesh of thee; and having restored the defiled image to its ancient estate, He hath suffused it with the divine beauty. Wherefore, confessing salvation, we set it forth in deed and word.

-35.title: Troparion of St. Gregory Palamas
-35.text: O light of Orthodoxy, support and teacher of the Church, beauty of monastics, invincible defender of theologians, O Gregory the Wonderworker, boast of Thessalonica, herald of grace: ever pray that our souls may be saved.

-35.title: Kontakion of St. Gregory Palamas
-35.text: Holy and divine instrument of wisdom, clarion of theology united in one accord, we hymn thee, O Gregory of divine speech. But, as a mind standing now before the Primal Mind, do thou ever direct our minds unto Him, that we may cry: Rejoice, O herald of grace.

-28.title: Troparion of the Veneration of the Cross
-28.text: O Lord, save Thy people, and bless Thine inheritance; grant Thou unto Orthodox Christians victory over their adversaries; and by the power of Thy Cross do Thou preserve Thy commonwealth.

-28.title: Kontakion of the Veneration of the Cross
-28.text: No longer doth the fiery sword guard the gate of Eden, for upon it hath come a strange undoing through the wood of the Cross. The sting hath been taken from death, the victory from hell; and Thou, my Saviour, art appeared, crying unto those in Hades: Enter again into Paradise.

-21.title: Troparion of St. John of the Ladder
-21.text: With the streams of thy tears thou didst cultivate the barren desert, and by thy deep sighing thou didst render fruitful thy labours an hundredfold; and thou becamest a luminary, shining throughout the world in miracles, O John, our righteous father. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

-21.title: Kontakion of St. John of the Ladder
-21.text: On the heights the Lord of the heavens hath truly set thee, as a star unwandering, enlightening the ends of the earth, O guide of fathers and teacher, O John our righteous one.

-14.title: Troparion of St. Mary of Egypt
-14.text: In thee, O Mother Mary, was preserved with exactness the image of God; for thou didst take up thy Cross and follow Christ. Thou didst teach by deed to disdain the flesh as transient, and to attend to the soul as a thing immortal. Wherefore thy spirit rejoiceth with the Angels.

-14.title: Kontakion of St. Mary of Egypt
-14.text: Having escaped the gloom of sin, O glorious one, and having enlightened thy heart with the light of repentance, thou didst come unto Christ and didst offer unto Him His all-blameless and holy Mother, as an intercessor of grace. Wherefore thou hast found forgiveness of transgressions and ever rejoicest with the Angels.

14.title: Troparion of the Myrrh-Bearing Women
14.text: The noble Joseph, taking Thine immaculate Body down from the Tree and having wrapped it in pure linen with sweet spices, laid it for burial in a new tomb. But on the third day Thou didst arise, O Lord, granting the world great mercy.

14.title: Kontakion of the Myrrh-Bearing Women
14.text: When Thou didst cry, Rejoice! unto the myrrh-bearers, Thou didst quench the lamentation of Eve, the first mother, by Thy Resurrection, O Christ God. And Thou didst bid Thine Apostles preach: The Saviour is risen from the tomb.

35.title: Troparion of the Sunday of the Blind Man
35.text: Let us, the faithful, praise and worship the Word, co-eternal with the Father and the Spirit, born of the Virgin for our salvation; for He took pleasure to ascend the Cross in the flesh, to endure death, and to raise the dead by His glorious Resurrection.

35.title: Kontakion of the Blind Man
35.text: Blinded in the eyes of my soul, I come unto Thee, O Christ, like the man blind from birth, and crying out in penitence I say: Thou art the Light most radiant of those in darkness.

42.title: Troparion of the Holy Fathers of the First Ecumenical Council
42.text: Most glorified art Thou, O Christ our God, who hast established the holy Fathers as luminaries upon the earth, and through them hast guided us all unto the true Faith. O greatly compassionate One, glory to Thee.

42.title: Kontakion of the Holy Fathers
42.text: The preaching of the Apostles and the doctrines of the Fathers confirmed one Faith for the Church; she, clothed with the garment of truth woven from theology on high, doth rightly divide the great mystery of piety, and giveth glory.

50.title: Troparion of the Day of the Holy Spirit
50.text: Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, who hast revealed the fishermen as most wise by sending down upon them the Holy Spirit, and through them drawing the world into Thy net. O Lover of mankind, glory to Thee.

50.title: Kontakion of the Holy Spirit
50.text: When the Most High came down and confused the tongues, He divided the nations; but when He distributed the tongues of fire, He called all unto unity; and with one accord we glorify the all-holy Spirit.

01-01.title: Troparion of the Circumcision
01-01.text: Without leaving the bosom of the Father, the Lord of all hath come down upon the earth, and from a holy Virgin, having put on our flesh and becoming subject to the Law, was circumcised on the eighth day. Wherefore Saint Basil, who in this day was named by thee, doth join us in praising thee.

01-01.title: Troparion of St. Basil the Great
01-01.text: Thy proclamation hath gone forth into all the earth, which was divinely taught by hearing thy voice. Thou didst expound the nature of creatures, ennoble the manners of men, and royal priesthood, O righteous Father. Pray to Christ God to save our souls.

01-01.title: Kontakion of the Circumcision and St. Basil
01-01.text: The Master of all endureth circumcision, and of His goodness cutteth away the transgressions of mortal men. On this day He granteth salvation unto the world, while in the highest there rejoiceth the hierarch and luminous priest of the Creator, the divine initiate of Christ, Basil.

01-02.title: Troparion of St. Seraphim of Sarov
01-02.text: From thy youth thou didst love Christ, O blessed one, and longing to work for Him alone, thou didst struggle in the wilderness with constant prayer and labor. With penitent heart and great love for Christ thou wast favored by the Mother of God. Wherefore we cry to thee: Save us by thy prayers, O Seraphim, our righteous Father.

01-02.title: Kontakion of St. Seraphim of Sarov
01-02.text: Having forsaken the beauty of the world and the things of corruption, O righteous one, thou didst settle in the monastery of Sarov, and didst there shine forth as an Angel; granting healings to the sick and revealing unto all the joy of the Resurrection, O Seraphim our father.

01-03.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Malachi; Martyr Gordius
01-03.text: The holy prophet Malachi; Martyr Gordius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

01-03.title: Kontakion of the Prophet Malachi
01-03.text: Having received the gift of prophecy, O Malachi, thou didst foretell unto the world the coming of Christ and the new sacrifice that shall be offered in every place. Wherefore we honour thy memory and cry: rejoice, O herald of the Sun of Righteousness.

01-04.title: Troparion of Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles; Forefeast of Theophany
01-04.text: O Holy Apostle Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles; Forefeast of Theophany, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

01-04.title: Kontakion of the Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles
01-04.text: Let us, the faithful, with hymns honour the chorus of the seventy disciples of Christ; for from them have we been taught to worship the undivided Trinity, and they abide as steadfast lamps of the Faith.

01-05.title: Troparion of Eve of Theophany — Strict Fast; Martyrs Theopempt and Theonas
01-05.text: Thy Martyr Eve of Theophany — Strict Fast; Martyrs Theopempt and Theonas, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

01-05.title: Kontakion of the Eve of Theophany
01-05.text: On this day, in the streams of the Jordan, the Lord crieth unto John: Fear not to baptize Me, for I am come to save Adam the firstformed.

01-06.title: Troparion of the Theophany
01-06.text: When Thou, O Lord, wast baptized in the Jordan, the worship of the Trinity was made manifest, for the voice of the Father gave witness to Thee, calling Thee His beloved Son. And the Spirit, in the form of a dove, confirmed the truthfulness of His word. O Christ our God, who hast appeared and enlightened the world, glory to Thee.

01-06.title: Kontakion of the Theophany
01-06.text: On this day Thou hast appeared unto the whole world, and Thy light, O Lord, hath been signed upon us, who with knowledge sing Thy praises: Thou hast come and revealed Thyself, the Light Unapproachable.

01-07.title: Troparion of the Forerunner
01-07.text: The just man is remembered with songs of praise, but for thee the testimony of the Lord is sufficient, O Forerunner. Truly thou wast revealed more honorable than the prophets, for thou wast deemed worthy to baptize in the Jordan Him whom they had preached.

01-07.title: Kontakion of the Forerunner
01-07.text: The river Jordan trembled and turned back, beholding the baptism of the Master; and John drew back his hand in fear. The hosts of Angels were amazed, seeing Thee in the flesh in the river. Wherefore we cry unto Thee: O Forerunner of the Lord, pray for us.

01-08.title: Troparion of St. George the Chozebite; St. Domnica
01-08.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. George the Chozebite; St. Domnica, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

01-08.title: Kontakion of St. George the Chozebite and St. Domnica
01-08.text: Having borne the cross of asceticism in the wilderness of Choziba, thou didst shine forth as a lamp upon the rocks, O George; and with thee we hymn the venerable Domnica, who quenched the passions with streams of tears.

01-09.title: Troparion of Martyr Polyeuctus of Melitene in Armenia
01-09.text: Thy Martyr Polyeuctus of Melitene in Armenia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

01-09.title: Kontakion of Martyr Polyeuctus
01-09.text: Having stripped off the idols of darkness and put on the armour of Christ, thou didst contend nobly, O Polyeuctus, and didst seal thy faith with thy blood. Wherefore we honour thee as a heavenly defender.

01-10.title: Troparion of St. Gregory of Nyssa
01-10.text: O God of our fathers, who dost ever deal with us according to Thy gentleness: take not Thy mercy from us, but by the intercessions of Gregory the hierarch, guide our life in peace.

01-10.title: Kontakion of St. Gregory of Nyssa
01-10.text: With the eye of thy mind ever fixed upon God, thou didst behold the depths of His mysteries, O Gregory, and didst pour forth upon the Church the streams of theology. Wherefore, O initiate of the Spirit, pray that our souls be saved.

01-11.title: Troparion of St. Theodosius the Great
01-11.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Theodosius the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

01-11.title: Kontakion of St. Theodosius the Great
01-11.text: Planted in the courts of the Lord, O father, thou didst blossom with the fruit of holiness, and didst gather thereunto a multitude of monastics, leading them upward by thy teaching. Wherefore we cry unto thee: rejoice, O father of cenobites, Theodosius.

01-12.title: Troparion of the Martyr Tatiana
01-12.text: Thy lamb Tatiana cries out to Thee, O Jesus, with a great voice: I love Thee, O my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I run to Thee. I am crucified with Thee and baptized into Thy death; I suffer for Thy sake that I may reign with Thee, and I die for Thee that I may live in Thee. By her prayers, as a spotless sacrifice, save our souls, O Thou who lovest mankind.

01-12.title: Kontakion of the Martyr Tatiana
01-12.text: Thou didst shine forth in martyrdom, O most holy Tatiana, having put to shame the cunning of the deceiver; and on the Cross thou didst behold Him whom thou didst love, and now thou rejoicest with the Bridegroom in the heavenly courts.

01-13.title: Troparion of Martyrs Hermylas and Stratonicus
01-13.text: Thy Martyrs Hermylas and Stratonicus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

01-13.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Hermylas and Stratonicus
01-13.text: Yoked together by the bond of love, ye contended valiantly, O Hermylas and Stratonicus, and didst receive the wreaths of martyrdom from the Master of all. Wherefore pray for us, who honour your memory.

01-14.title: Troparion of St. Nina of Georgia
01-14.text: O handmaid of the Word of God, who in preaching did equal the first-called Andrew and the other Apostles, the enlightener of Iberia and reed-pipe of the Holy Spirit, holy Equal-to-the-Apostles Nina, pray to Christ God to save our souls.

01-14.title: Kontakion of St. Nina of Georgia
01-14.text: Equal-to-the-Apostles Nina, by the vine of thy hair Christ did call thee, and by it thou didst water Iberia with the streams of the Faith. Wherefore we hymn thee as the enlightener of the Georgian land.

01-15.title: Troparion of St. Paul of Thebes
01-15.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Paul of Thebes, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

01-15.title: Kontakion of St. Paul of Thebes
01-15.text: Like a lamp set upon a stand, thou didst illumine the desert, O Paul, and by thy fasts didst put to flight the legions of the enemy. Wherefore we honour thee as the first dweller of the inner wilderness.

01-16.title: Troparion of Veneration of the Honorable Chains of the Apostle Peter
01-16.text: O Holy Apostle Veneration of the Honorable Chains of the Apostle Peter, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

01-16.title: Kontakion of the Veneration of Peter's Chains
01-16.text: Christ the rock doth glorify with all glory the rock of faith, the chief of the disciples; and He doth summon us together to hymn the wonders of his chains, granting forgiveness of transgressions.

01-17.title: Troparion of St. Anthony the Great
01-17.text: Emulating the ways of Elijah the zealot, and following the straight paths of John the Baptist, O Father Anthony, thou didst become a dweller of the wilderness and didst confirm the world by thy prayers. Wherefore intercede with Christ God that our souls be saved.

01-17.title: Kontakion of St. Anthony the Great
01-17.text: Having forsaken the tumult of the world, thou didst end thy life in asceticism, O most blessed Anthony, becoming an imitator of the Baptist in every way; and with him we honour thee as the father of fathers.

01-18.title: Troparion of Sts. Athanasius and Cyril of Alexandria
01-18.text: O works of piety and lights of Orthodoxy, you both shone forth as defenders of the truth — by your divine teaching you scattered the darkness of heresy. Pray to Christ God, O wise Athanasius and Cyril, that He may grant us great mercy.

01-18.title: Kontakion of Sts. Athanasius and Cyril
01-18.text: Hierarchs most great of piety, and noble champions of the Church of Christ, do ye preserve them that praise you, and protect from every snare them that hymn you with faith, O Athanasius and Cyril, ye initiates of God.

01-19.title: Troparion of the Venerable Macarius the Great
01-19.text: Citizen of the desert and angel in flesh, wonderworker and bearer of God: O Macarius our Father, who by fasts and watchings and prayers didst receive heavenly gifts to heal the sick and the souls of those who in faith run to thee. Glory to Him who hath given thee strength; glory to Him who hath crowned thee; glory to Him who through thee worketh wonders.

01-19.title: Kontakion of St. Macarius the Great
01-19.text: In the house of God hast thou shown thyself as an olive tree fruitful in piety, O father; for in the wilderness thou didst gather the harvest of holiness, gladdening the souls of those that honour thee.

01-20.title: Troparion of St. Euthymius the Great
01-20.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Euthymius the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

01-20.title: Kontakion of St. Euthymius the Great
01-20.text: In thy honoured nativity, O father, the wilderness rejoiced; and by thy departure, all the world hath received joy. Wherefore we hymn thee as a divine luminary of monastics, O Euthymius.

01-21.title: Troparion of St. Maximus the Confessor
01-21.text: Champion of Orthodoxy, teacher of purity and of true worship, the enlightener of the universe and the adornment of hierarchs, all-wise father Maximus, thy teachings have gleamed forth on all things like the rays of the sun, intercede with Christ God to save our souls.

01-21.title: Kontakion of St. Maximus the Confessor
01-21.text: Trumpet of theology, sound forth, declaring the mysteries hidden from eternity; and by thy sufferings, O Maximus, thou didst confess Christ as one Person in two natures, thus stopping the mouths of heretics.

01-22.title: Troparion of Apostle Timothy of the Seventy; Martyr Anastasius the Persian
01-22.text: The holy apostle Timothy of the Seventy; Martyr Anastasius the Persian, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

01-22.title: Kontakion of Apostle Timothy and Martyr Anastasius
01-22.text: Let us, the faithful, hymn the divine disciple of Paul, even Timothy, together with the steadfast Anastasius the Persian; for they have inherited the kingdom on high, and ever pray that our souls may be saved.

01-23.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Clement of Ancyra; Martyr Agathangelus
01-23.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Clement of Ancyra; Martyr Agathangelus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

01-23.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Clement
01-23.text: Like a fruitful vine in the courts of the Lord, thou didst pour forth the wine of confession, O Clement, hierarch of Ancyra; and watered by thy blood didst spring up the seeds of the Faith.

01-24.title: Troparion of Blessed Xenia of St. Petersburg
01-24.text: Having renounced the vanity of the earthly world, thou didst take up the cross of a homeless life of wandering. Thou didst not fear grief, privation, nor the mockery of men, and didst know the love of Christ; nourished by it, now in heavenly joy, O Blessed Xenia, pray to Christ God that our souls may be saved.

01-24.title: Kontakion of Blessed Xenia of St. Petersburg
01-24.text: Having taken upon thee the cross of foolishness for Christ, thou didst tread the streets of the city as a stranger, O Xenia, and from the heights dost now bestow consolation upon all that flee unto thee.

01-25.title: Troparion of St. Gregory the Theologian
01-25.text: The pastoral pipe of thy theology overcame the trumpets of the rhetoricians, for the depths of the Spirit having been searched out by thee, the beauty of the word was added to thee. Intercede with Christ God, O Father Gregory, that our souls be saved.

01-25.title: Kontakion of St. Gregory the Theologian
01-25.text: With thy theological tongue thou didst loose the tangled webs of the orators, O glorious one; and didst clothe the Church Orthodox in the vesture of true confession, which she putteth not off, but rejoiceth in, theologizing with us, of thy mystic intercession.

01-26.title: Troparion of St. Xenophon
01-26.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Xenophon, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

01-26.title: Kontakion of St. Xenophon and His Family
01-26.text: Like Abraham of old, O Xenophon, with thy wife Maria and thy sons, thou didst forsake the world and find the city of God; wherein ye all rejoice with the choir of the venerable.

01-27.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of St. John Chrysostom
01-27.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Translation of the Relics of St. John Chrysostom, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

01-27.title: Kontakion of the Translation of St. John Chrysostom
01-27.text: The Church of Christ rejoiceth at the return of thy honoured relics, O Chrysostom, and the city of Constantine receiveth thee with gladness; for thou pourest forth, as ever, the gold of thy teaching upon the faithful.

01-28.title: Troparion of St. Ephraim the Syrian
01-28.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Ephraim the Syrian, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

01-28.title: Kontakion of St. Ephraim the Syrian
01-28.text: Ever mindful of the hour of judgment, thou didst weep most bitterly, O Ephraim, while in deed thou wast a guide and teacher. Wherefore, O universal father, thou dost stir up the slothful unto repentance.

01-29.title: Troparion of the Translation of the Relics of St. Ignatius the God-bearer
01-29.text: Sharing the way of life of the Apostles, and successor to their throne, inspired by God, thou didst find the way to divine contemplation through the practice of virtue. Wherefore, ministering the Word of truth without error, thou didst contend for the Faith even to the shedding of thy blood. O hieromartyr Ignatius, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

01-29.title: Kontakion of the Translation of St. Ignatius
01-29.text: Antioch is glad at the return of thy relics, O wheat ground beneath the teeth of the beasts; and we, beholding thee as a pure loaf set upon the table of the Master, hymn thee, O Ignatius the God-bearer.

01-30.title: Troparion of the Three Hierarchs
01-30.text: As the Apostles equal in honor and the teachers of the world, intercede with the Master of all to grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

01-30.title: Kontakion of the Three Hierarchs
01-30.text: Thou hast taken to Thyself, O Lord, the divinely-eloquent and sacred heralds, the foremost teachers, into the enjoyment of Thy good things and into Thy rest: for Thou hast accepted their labors and their death above every offering, O Thou who alone glorifiest Thy saints.

01-31.title: Troparion of Holy Unmercenaries and Wonderworkers Cyrus and John
01-31.text: O Wonderworker Holy Unmercenaries and Wonderworkers Cyrus and John, thou wast filled with grace from on high and didst pour out unto the faithful the gift of healing. Pray Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

01-31.title: Kontakion of Unmercenaries Cyrus and John
01-31.text: Having received the gift of healings, ye bestow healing upon the sick without payment, O glorious wonderworkers Cyrus and John; for ye take not silver, but pour forth health upon all that flee unto your shrine.

02-01.title: Troparion of the Martyr Tryphon
02-01.text: Thy Martyr Tryphon, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from thee, our God; for, having thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

02-01.title: Kontakion of the Martyr Tryphon
02-01.text: Adorned with the threefold radiance of thy contests, thou hast appeared as a luminary of the world, O glorious Tryphon; and now in the heavens thou dost intercede with the Master for them that hymn thy memory.

02-02.title: Troparion of the Meeting of the Lord
02-02.text: Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, full of grace, for from thee hath risen the Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God, enlightening those who sat in darkness. Be glad also, O righteous Elder, for thou didst bear in thine arms the Liberator of our souls, who granteth us also the resurrection.

02-02.title: Kontakion of the Meeting of the Lord
02-02.text: Thou who by Thy birth didst sanctify the womb of the Virgin, and who didst bless the hands of Simeon as was meet, by anticipation hast even now saved us, O Christ God. But give peace in the midst of wars unto Thy commonwealth, and strengthen the rulers whom Thou hast loved, O only Lover of mankind.

02-03.title: Troparion of Righteous Symeon the God-receiver and Anna the Prophetess
02-03.text: Great are the achievements of faith! O Righteous Righteous Symeon the God-receiver and Anna the Prophetess, thou didst shine like a star upon the earth, leaving us a model of holy life. Through thy prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

02-03.title: Kontakion of Righteous Symeon and Anna
02-03.text: Thou didst rejoice, O elder Symeon, embracing in thine arms the Liberator of our souls who granteth us also the resurrection; and Anna the Prophetess hymned with thee the Saviour of the world.

02-04.title: Troparion of St. Isidore of Pelusium
02-04.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Isidore of Pelusium, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

02-04.title: Kontakion of St. Isidore of Pelusium
02-04.text: With the rays of thy letters thou didst illumine the Church, O Isidore, casting forth the gloom of error; for thou wast a treasury of divine wisdom and a fountain of monastic counsel.

02-05.title: Troparion of the Martyr Agatha
02-05.text: Thy lamb Agatha, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking thee I contest. I am crucified with thee, and in thy baptism am buried with thee. I suffer for thy sake, that I might reign with thee; I die for thee, that I might live in thee. By her prayers save our souls.

02-05.title: Kontakion of the Martyr Agatha
02-05.text: Let the Church be crowned today with garlands of praise for Agatha, the all-glorious bride of Christ, who cried out: For Thy love, O Saviour, I count not my flesh as anything.

02-06.title: Troparion of St. Photius the Great
02-06.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Photius the Great, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

02-06.title: Kontakion of St. Photius the Great
02-06.text: O hierarch most wise and pillar of Orthodoxy, who didst confound the errors of the Latins, O Photius, do thou ever pray that the Church may be preserved unshaken in the true Faith.

02-07.title: Troparion of St. Parthenius
02-07.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Parthenius, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

02-07.title: Kontakion of St. Parthenius
02-07.text: Filled with the grace of healing, O hierarch, thou didst cast out demons and cleanse the sick, O Parthenius bishop of Lampsacus; wherefore we honour thy memory with hymns.

02-08.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Theodore the General
02-08.text: Resplendent in the bravery of thy soul and the truth of thy faith, holy great-martyr Theodore, thou didst contend with valor; before thy passion thou didst slay the dragon with the spear of the Cross. Wherefore intercede with Christ God for our souls.

02-08.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Theodore the General
02-08.text: Imitating the courage of thy faith, thou didst not bow down before the idols, O Theodore the General; and so thou didst receive the wreath of martyrdom from Christ the great King.

02-09.title: Troparion of Martyr Nicephorus of Antioch
02-09.text: Thy Martyr Nicephorus of Antioch, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

02-09.title: Kontakion of Martyr Nicephorus
02-09.text: Confessing the bond of love above all else, O Nicephorus, thou didst surrender thy head for Christ in place of thy quarrel; and we honour thee as a teacher of brotherly peace.

02-10.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Charalampias the Wonderworker
02-10.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Charalampias the Wonderworker, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

02-10.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Charalampias
02-10.text: Thou didst shine forth as a pillar of unshaken faith, O Charalampias, and through the sufferings of thy flesh didst confound the deceiver. Wherefore the Church doth crown thee with hymns.

02-11.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Blaise
02-11.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Blaise, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

02-11.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Blaise
02-11.text: As a divine companion of the martyrs, O Blaise, and as a champion of the unwavering Faith, thou didst find God a perfecter of thy contests; wherefore we who run unto thee receive healing and salvation.

02-12.title: Troparion of St. Meletius
02-12.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Meletius, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

02-12.title: Kontakion of St. Meletius of Antioch
02-12.text: Faithful pastor and divinely-wise teacher, thou didst hierarchically govern the Church of Antioch, O Meletius; and from thy hands the great Chrysostom received the holy oil of unction.

02-13.title: Troparion of St. Martinianus the Hermit
02-13.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Martinianus the Hermit, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

02-13.title: Kontakion of St. Martinianus the Hermit
02-13.text: Having fled the snares of the harlot by leaping into the flames, thou didst show thyself a true ascetic, O Martinianus; and from the depths of the sea God brought thee forth, that thy struggles might be known to all.

02-14.title: Troparion of St. Cyril
02-14.text: O Holy Apostle St. Cyril, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

02-14.title: Kontakion of St. Cyril, Equal-to-the-Apostles
02-14.text: Thou didst impart the Faith unto the Slavs, O Cyril, by means of the letters thou didst craft; and so thou abidest as the enlightener of nations that knew not the Word.

02-15.title: Troparion of Apostle Onesimus of the Seventy
02-15.text: O Holy Apostle Onesimus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

02-15.title: Kontakion of Apostle Onesimus
02-15.text: From slavery thou didst become a freedman of Christ, O Onesimus, and from a runaway didst become a beloved disciple of Paul; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee as a profitable servant.

02-16.title: Troparion of Martyrs Pamphilus of Caesarea and his twelve companions
02-16.text: Thy Martyrs Pamphilus of Caesarea and his twelve companions, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

02-16.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Pamphilus and Companions
02-16.text: As a chorus of twelve, ye contended for the Faith and didst trample upon the deceits of the idolaters, O Pamphilus and ye companions; wherefore we hymn your steadfast confession.

02-17.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro
02-17.text: Great are the achievements of faith! In the fountain of flame, as in refreshing water, the holy martyr Theodore rejoiced; for, having been wholly consumed as sweet-smelling bread by the fire, he was offered as a precious sacrifice unto the Holy Trinity. By his intercessions, O Christ God, save our souls.

02-17.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro
02-17.text: Having received the faith of Christ within thy heart as a breastplate, thou didst tread under foot the hosts of the enemy, O great-martyr, and wast crowned eternally with the heavenly wreath as one invincible.

02-18.title: Troparion of St. Leo the Great
02-18.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Leo the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

02-18.title: Kontakion of St. Leo the Great of Rome
02-18.text: Thou didst sit upon the chief see of Peter, O Leo, and didst proclaim Christ in two natures, casting down the heresy of Eutyches; and thy Tome remaineth the rule of the Faith.

02-19.title: Troparion of Apostle Archippus of the Seventy
02-19.text: O Holy Apostle Archippus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

02-19.title: Kontakion of Apostle Archippus
02-19.text: Like a fellow-labourer of Paul, O Archippus, thou didst pour forth the seeds of preaching upon Colossae; and now thou prayest unceasingly for them that honour thee.

02-20.title: Troparion of St. Leo
02-20.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Leo, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

02-20.title: Kontakion of St. Leo of Catania
02-20.text: Beholding the prodigies of the sorcerer, thou didst confound him by the sign of the Cross, O Leo; and didst shine forth as a wonderworker in the city of Catania.

02-21.title: Troparion of St. Timothy of Symbola
02-21.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Timothy of Symbola, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

02-21.title: Kontakion of St. Timothy of Symbola
02-21.text: Like a tree planted by the streams of asceticism, thou didst yield the fruit of holiness, O Timothy of Symbola; wherefore we hymn thee as a guide unto the heavenly hills.

02-22.title: Troparion of Finding of the Relics of the Holy Martyrs at the Gate of Eugenius
02-22.text: Thy Martyr Finding of the Relics of the Holy Martyrs at the Gate of Eugenius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

02-22.title: Kontakion of the Martyrs at the Gate of Eugenius
02-22.text: Hidden for many years, your relics, O holy martyrs, now shine forth as a fountain of healings unto the city of Constantine; wherefore we glorify Christ who hath glorified you.

02-23.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Polycarp of Smyrna
02-23.text: Thou wast a companion of the apostles in their way of life and didst occupy their thrones, O divinely-inspired bishop; through the practice of virtues thou didst attain to the height of divine contemplation. Imparting the word of truth without error, thou didst contest for the faith even to the shedding of thy blood. O Hieromartyr Polycarp, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

02-23.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Polycarp
02-23.text: Thou didst draw fruit unending for the Lord, O blessed Polycarp; for thou didst show thyself a teacher of piety and a hieromartyr of true confession, sealing it in the fire.

02-24.title: Troparion of First and Second Findings of the Honorable Head of John the Baptist
02-24.text: O holy First and Second Findings of the Honorable Head of John the Baptist, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

02-24.title: Kontakion of the Finding of the Forerunner's Head
02-24.text: Once hidden in the earth, thy honoured head, O Forerunner, hath shone forth as the dawn of healings, and proclaimed afar the radiance of repentance, with which it summoneth the world.

02-25.title: Troparion of St. Tarasius
02-25.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Tarasius, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

02-25.title: Kontakion of St. Tarasius
02-25.text: With the wisdom of the Spirit thou didst confound the iconoclasts, O Tarasius, and didst restore the veneration of the sacred images; wherefore we glorify thee, hierarch of God.

02-26.title: Troparion of the Equal-to-the-Apostles Photini
02-26.text: Illumined by the Holy Spirit, O Photini, thou didst drink with delight the water from Christ which He giveth unto those who seek; thou didst share the streams of immortality with thy people. Wherefore we honor thy contest, Great-martyr equal to the Apostles, and entreat thee to pray for the salvation of our souls.

02-26.title: Kontakion of the Equal-to-the-Apostles Photini
02-26.text: Having drawn from the well the Water of Life, O Photini, thou didst preach Christ unto thy people and didst seal thy witness with martyrdom; wherefore we honour thee as enlightener of many.

02-27.title: Troparion of St. Procopius the Confessor of Decapolis
02-27.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Procopius the Confessor of Decapolis, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

02-27.title: Kontakion of St. Procopius of Decapolis
02-27.text: Having contended for the icons of Christ, O Procopius, thou didst endure tortures with a noble mind, and so didst earn the title Confessor; wherefore we crown thee with hymns.

02-28.title: Troparion of St. Basil the Confessor
02-28.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Basil the Confessor, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

02-28.title: Kontakion of St. Basil the Confessor
02-28.text: Yokefellow of Procopius in the defense of the holy icons, O Basil, thou didst share his confession and his crown; wherefore we hymn you both with one voice.

03-01.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Eudokia of Heliopolis
03-01.text: Thy lamb Eudokia of Heliopolis, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

03-01.title: Kontakion of Virgin-martyr Eudokia
03-01.text: Once fallen, thou didst rise by the grace of Christ, and didst run through the contest of asceticism, O Eudokia; and at the last, sealing thy life with the blood of martyrdom, thou wast crowned in the heavens.

03-02.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Theodotus
03-02.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Theodotus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

03-02.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Theodotus
03-02.text: Like a true shepherd thou didst guard thy flock of Cyrenia, O Theodotus, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the blood of martyrdom; wherefore we honour thee as an unshaken pillar.

03-03.title: Troparion of Martyrs Eutropius
03-03.text: Thy Martyrs Eutropius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-03.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus
03-03.text: Joined together by the bond of love, ye contended for Christ, O Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus; and ye received the wreaths of victory from the hand of the Master.

03-04.title: Troparion of St. Gerasimus of the Jordan
03-04.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Gerasimus of the Jordan, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

03-04.title: Kontakion of St. Gerasimus of the Jordan
03-04.text: Beside the streams of the Jordan thou didst plant the garden of asceticism, O Gerasimus, and even the lion of the wilderness was tamed by thee; wherefore we hymn thee as a peaceable father.

03-05.title: Troparion of Martyr Conon the Gardener of Isauria
03-05.text: Thy Martyr Conon the Gardener of Isauria, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-05.title: Kontakion of Martyr Conon
03-05.text: Like a husbandman cultivating the field of Christ, O Conon, thou didst water it with thy blood; and the harvest of thy contest doth feed the souls of the faithful.

03-06.title: Troparion of The Forty-two Martyrs of Amorion
03-06.text: Thy Martyr The Forty-two Martyrs of Amorion, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-06.title: Kontakion of the Forty-two Martyrs of Amorion
03-06.text: Having been led captive but unconquered, O ye forty-two soldiers, ye refused to deny Christ before the unbelievers, and so received the wreaths of martyrdom in Samarra.

03-07.title: Troparion of The Hieromartyrs of Cherson (Basil
03-07.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired The Hieromartyrs of Cherson (Basil, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

03-07.title: Kontakion of the Hieromartyrs of Cherson
03-07.text: Having watered the city of Cherson with the streams of preaching and with your blood, O ye seven hierarchs, ye made the wilderness of unbelief blossom; wherefore the Church doth bless your memory.

03-08.title: Troparion of St. Theophylact the Confessor
03-08.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Theophylact the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

03-08.title: Kontakion of St. Theophylact the Confessor
03-08.text: Confessing the icons of Christ, thou didst endure exile and reproach, O Theophylact; and from the heights thou prayest for them that hymn thy contest.

03-09.title: Troparion of the Forty Martyrs
03-09.text: Through the sufferings which Thy holy Forty Martyrs endured for Thy sake, O Lord, we beseech Thee, O Lover of mankind: heal all our infirmities.

03-09.title: Kontakion of the Forty Martyrs of Sebaste
03-09.text: Having forsaken every army of this world, ye cleaved unto the Master in the heavens, O ye forty victorious athletes; and through the icy waters of Sebaste ye received the wreaths of unfading glory.

03-10.title: Troparion of Martyr Quadratus and his companions at Corinth
03-10.text: Thy Martyr Quadratus and his companions at Corinth, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-10.title: Kontakion of Martyr Quadratus and Companions
03-10.text: Like a sacred band ye contended at Corinth, O Quadratus and ye companions, and ye received from the hand of the Master the wreaths of martyrdom; wherefore the Church doth crown your memory.

03-11.title: Troparion of St. Sophronius
03-11.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Sophronius, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

03-11.title: Kontakion of St. Sophronius of Jerusalem
03-11.text: From the holy city thou didst send forth the streams of true confession, O Sophronius, and didst rebuke the monothelite error; wherefore we hymn thee as guardian of Orthodoxy.

03-12.title: Troparion of Symeon the New Theologian
03-12.text: Receiving the illumination of the Holy Spirit, O most blessed Symeon, thou wast revealed as a chosen vessel and beheld the Sun of Glory at thy side. The Divine Light that shone upon thee thou hast given to us in thy writings; we entreat thee, intercede that we also may be illumined and may attain salvation.

03-12.title: Kontakion of St. Symeon the New Theologian
03-12.text: Thou wast filled with the divine light, O Symeon, and the eyes of thy soul did behold God Himself; and now from the heights thou dost teach us, theologizing of divine illumination.

03-13.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of St. Nicephorus
03-13.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Translation of the Relics of St. Nicephorus, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

03-13.title: Kontakion of the Translation of St. Nicephorus
03-13.text: Returned in glory to the city of Constantine, O Nicephorus, thy relics shine forth as the icon of victory over iconoclasm; and we honour thy memory with hymns.

03-14.title: Troparion of St. Benedict of Nursia
03-14.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Benedict of Nursia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

03-14.title: Kontakion of St. Benedict of Nursia
03-14.text: Having forsaken the city of Rome for the cave of asceticism, O Benedict, thou didst draw together a chorus of monastics, giving them the Rule of holiness; wherefore the West doth honour thee as father of monks.

03-15.title: Troparion of Martyr Agapius and his seven companions at Caesarea
03-15.text: Thy Martyr Agapius and his seven companions at Caesarea, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-15.title: Kontakion of Martyr Agapius and Companions
03-15.text: With seven brethren in Christ thou didst contend at Caesarea, O Agapius, and didst receive the wreath that doth not fade; wherefore we glorify Him who hath crowned you.

03-16.title: Troparion of Martyr Sabinus of Egypt
03-16.text: Thy Martyr Sabinus of Egypt, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-16.title: Kontakion of Martyr Sabinus
03-16.text: Cast into the river yet unsinking by the prayer of faith, O Sabinus, thou didst contend nobly for Christ in the land of Egypt, and didst receive the crown of glory.

03-17.title: Troparion of St. Alexis the Man of God
03-17.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Alexis the Man of God, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

03-17.title: Kontakion of St. Alexis the Man of God
03-17.text: Forsaking the house of thy father, thou didst settle as a stranger, O Alexis, and didst live unknown in the very place of thy birth; wherefore the heavens proclaim what man knew not.

03-18.title: Troparion of St. Cyril
03-18.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Cyril, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

03-18.title: Kontakion of St. Cyril of Jerusalem
03-18.text: With the Catechetical Lectures thou didst guide thy flock unto the streams of the mysteries, O Cyril; wherefore we hymn thee as illuminator of catechumens.

03-19.title: Troparion of Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria
03-19.text: Thy Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-19.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria
03-19.text: Joined together by the unbreakable bond of Christian wedlock, O Chrysanthus and Daria, ye contended unto blood, and received the wreaths of martyrdom undivided.

03-20.title: Troparion of The Holy Fathers slain at the monastery of St. Sabbas
03-20.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father The Holy Fathers slain at the monastery of St. Sabbas, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

03-20.title: Kontakion of the Holy Fathers of St. Sabbas Monastery
03-20.text: Watered by your blood, the lavra of St. Sabbas hath sprung up a garden of righteousness, O ye Fathers; and ye intercede with Christ for them that honour your memory.

03-21.title: Troparion of St. James the Confessor
03-21.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father James the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

03-21.title: Kontakion of St. James the Confessor
03-21.text: Confessing the venerable icons, O James, thou didst endure exile and stripes, and didst seal thy faith with sufferings; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee as confessor.

03-22.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Basil
03-22.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Basil, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

03-22.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Basil of Ancyra
03-22.text: Like a sacred lamp of the Church, thou didst shine in Ancyra, O Basil, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the blood of martyrdom; wherefore we honour thee with hymns.

03-23.title: Troparion of Martyr Nicon of Sicily and his hundred and ninety-nine disciples
03-23.text: Thy Martyr Nicon of Sicily and his hundred and ninety-nine disciples, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-23.title: Kontakion of Martyr Nicon and Companions
03-23.text: With a great company of disciples thou didst seal thy faith in Sicily, O Nicon, and didst lead an hundred and ninety-nine unto the heavenly bridechamber; wherefore we hymn thy noble flock.

03-24.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
03-24.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

03-24.title: Kontakion of the Forefeast of the Annunciation
03-24.text: Today the Virgin standeth before the temple, and chasteneth her flesh by prayer; and Gabriel descendeth from the heavens, bringing unto her the joyous tidings of the salvation of the world.

03-25.title: Troparion of the Annunciation
03-25.text: Today is the beginning of our salvation, the revelation of the eternal mystery! The Son of God becomes the Son of the Virgin as Gabriel announces the coming of Grace. Together with him let us cry to the Theotokos: Rejoice, O Full of Grace, the Lord is with thee!

03-25.title: Kontakion of the Annunciation
03-25.text: To thee the Champion Leader we, thy servants, dedicate a feast of victory and thanksgiving as ones rescued out of sufferings, O Theotokos: but as thou art one with might which is invincible, from all dangers that can be, do thou deliver us, that we may cry unto thee: Rejoice, O Bride unwedded.

03-26.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
03-26.text: Captains of the heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy beseech thee that by your supplications thou wilt encompass us beneath the wings of thy immaterial glory, and faithfully preserve us who fall down and cry to thee: deliver us from all dangers, for ye are the commanders of the powers on high.

03-26.title: Kontakion of the Synaxis of the Archangel Gabriel
03-26.text: Foremost of the heavenly hosts, thou didst proclaim unto the Virgin the descent of the Word, O Gabriel; and now thou intercedest unceasingly for them that honour thy synaxis.

03-27.title: Troparion of Martyr Matrona of Thessalonica
03-27.text: Thy Martyr Matrona of Thessalonica, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

03-27.title: Kontakion of Martyr Matrona of Thessalonica
03-27.text: Slain by thine own mistress for the love of Christ, O Matrona, thou didst find the heavenly Mistress, the Theotokos, and a place in the bridechamber of the Lamb.

03-28.title: Troparion of St. Hilarion the New
03-28.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Hilarion the New, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

03-28.title: Kontakion of St. Hilarion the New
03-28.text: Renewing in thy life the labours of the ancient Hilarion, thou didst shine forth in Pelekete, O father; and the icons of Christ thou didst defend by thy confession.

03-29.title: Troparion of Hieromartyrs Mark of Arethusa and Cyril the Deacon
03-29.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyrs Mark of Arethusa and Cyril the Deacon, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

03-29.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyrs Mark and Cyril
03-29.text: As shepherds true unto thy flock, O Mark of Arethusa and Cyril the deacon, ye laid down your lives for the Faith, and so received the wreath of victory.

03-30.title: Troparion of St. John Climacus
03-30.text: Dweller of the desert and angel in the body, thou wast shown to be a wonderworker, our God-bearing father John. Thou didst receive heavenly gifts through fasting, vigil, and prayer: healing the sick and the souls of those drawn to thee by faith. Glory to Him that gave thee strength; glory to Him that crowned thee; glory to Him that worketh healings for all through thee.

03-30.title: Kontakion of St. John Climacus
03-30.text: Like a heavenly Climax thou didst raise up the souls of monastics, O John, by the Ladder of thy words; wherefore we ascend unto God by thy guidance.

03-31.title: Troparion of St. Innocent of Alaska
03-31.text: O Holy Father Innocent, with patience thou didst go forth into the wilds of the north, taking with thee the saving gospel; thou didst love thy spiritual children with the love of an apostle, working signs and wonders for their salvation. To us also has thy intercession been given in time of need. Wherefore we cry to thee: glory to Him who has given thee strength; glory to Him who hath crowned thee; glory to Him who through thee shineth in the lands of America.

03-31.title: Kontakion of St. Innocent of Alaska
03-31.text: Apostle of the northern lands, O Innocent, thou didst translate the Scriptures into the tongues of the Aleuts and Tlingits; and now from on high thou dost pray for the church thou didst plant.

04-01.title: Troparion of St. Mary of Egypt
04-01.text: The image of God was truly preserved in thee, O Mother, for thou didst take up the Cross and follow Christ. By thy actions thou didst teach us to despise the flesh, for it passeth away, but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. Wherefore thy spirit, O holy Mother Mary, rejoiceth with the angels.

04-01.title: Kontakion of St. Mary of Egypt
04-01.text: Having escaped the gloom of sin, O glorious one, and having enlightened thy heart with the light of repentance, thou didst come unto Christ; wherefore thou hast found forgiveness of transgressions and ever rejoicest with the Angels.

04-02.title: Troparion of St. Titus the Wonderworker
04-02.text: O Wonderworker Titus the Wonderworker, thou wast filled with grace from on high and didst pour out unto the faithful the gift of healing. Pray Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

04-02.title: Kontakion of St. Titus the Wonderworker
04-02.text: From thy youth hast thou taken up the yoke of asceticism, O Titus, and didst pour forth wonders upon the faithful; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a divine wonderworker.

04-03.title: Troparion of St. Nicetas the Confessor
04-03.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Nicetas the Confessor, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

04-03.title: Kontakion of St. Nicetas the Confessor
04-03.text: Like a noble champion thou didst stand for the icons of Christ, O Nicetas of Medikion, and didst endure exile and stripes; wherefore the Church crowneth thee with hymns.

04-04.title: Troparion of St. Joseph the Hymnographer
04-04.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Joseph the Hymnographer, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

04-04.title: Kontakion of St. Joseph the Hymnographer
04-04.text: Thou didst pour forth canons of supplication unto the saints, O Joseph, like a sweet-sounding lyre of the Spirit; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

04-05.title: Troparion of Martyrs Theodulus the Reader and Agathopodes the Deacon
04-05.text: Thy Martyrs Theodulus the Reader and Agathopodes the Deacon, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

04-05.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Theodulus and Agathopodes
04-05.text: Yoked together by the bond of love, ye contended unto the streams of martyrdom, O Theodulus and Agathopodes; wherefore the Church doth crown your memory with hymns.

04-06.title: Troparion of St. Eutychius
04-06.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Eutychius, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

04-06.title: Kontakion of St. Eutychius of Constantinople
04-06.text: Confessing the venerable icons and condemning the false teachings, thou didst shepherd the New Rome, O Eutychius; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee as a noble hierarch.

04-07.title: Troparion of St. George the Confessor
04-07.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father George the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

04-07.title: Kontakion of St. George of Mytilene
04-07.text: For the love of the holy icons thou didst endure exile from thy see, O George of Mytilene; and so thou didst attain the wreath of confession in the heavens.

04-08.title: Troparion of Apostles Herodion
04-08.text: O Holy Apostles Herodion, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

04-08.title: Kontakion of Apostles Herodion and Companions
04-08.text: Like a sacred sixfold chorus of apostles ye preached Christ unto the nations; and we honour you, O Herodion, Agabus, Rufus, Asyncritus, Phlegon, and Hermes.

04-09.title: Troparion of Martyr Eupsychius of Caesarea in Cappadocia
04-09.text: Thy Martyr Eupsychius of Caesarea in Cappadocia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

04-09.title: Kontakion of Martyr Eupsychius
04-09.text: Having broken the idols of paganism in Caesarea, thou didst seal thy boldness with thy blood, O Eupsychius; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a great martyr.

04-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Terence
04-10.text: Thy Martyrs Terence, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

04-10.title: Kontakion of the Martyrs of Africa
04-10.text: Like seven steadfast pillars of the Faith, ye contended in Carthage, O Terence and ye companions, and ye sealed your confession in the blood of martyrdom.

04-11.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Antipas of Pergamum
04-11.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Antipas of Pergamum, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

04-11.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Antipas of Pergamum
04-11.text: Thou didst pour forth wonders unto the faithful, O Antipas, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the brazen bull; wherefore the Lord did glorify thy memory with healings.

04-12.title: Troparion of St. Basil the Confessor
04-12.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Basil the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

04-12.title: Kontakion of St. Basil of Parium
04-12.text: Confessing the icons of Christ before tyrants, O Basil of Parium, thou didst endure stripes and exile, sealing thy faith with steadfast confession.

04-13.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Artemon
04-13.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Artemon, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

04-13.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Artemon
04-13.text: As a chosen vessel of the Lord, thou didst water Laodicea with the streams of preaching, O Artemon; and thou didst seal thy presbytery with the blood of martyrdom.

04-14.title: Troparion of St. Martin the Confessor
04-14.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Martin the Confessor, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

04-14.title: Kontakion of St. Martin of Rome
04-14.text: Confessing two wills in the one Christ, thou didst stand alone against the heretics, O Martin Pope of Rome, and didst die in exile at Cherson; wherefore the Church doth crown thee as confessor.

04-15.title: Troparion of Apostles Aristarchus
04-15.text: O Holy Apostles Aristarchus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

04-15.title: Kontakion of Apostles Aristarchus and Companions
04-15.text: Disciples and fellow-labourers of Paul, ye spread the seed of preaching among the nations, O Aristarchus, Pudens, and Trophimus; wherefore the Church doth hymn your memory.

04-16.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyrs Agape
04-16.text: Thy lamb Virgin-martyrs Agape, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

04-16.title: Kontakion of Virgin-martyrs Agape, Irene, and Chionia
04-16.text: Like a triple lampstand of the Faith, ye lit up Aquileia, O Agape, Irene, and Chionia; and ye sealed your virginity with the wreaths of martyrdom.

04-17.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Symeon of Persia
04-17.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Symeon of Persia, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

04-17.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Symeon of Persia
04-17.text: Refusing to bow before the sun, O Symeon, thou didst lead a great chorus of martyrs unto Christ in Persia; and ye received together the wreaths of victory.

04-18.title: Troparion of St. John
04-18.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. John, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

04-18.title: Kontakion of St. John, disciple of St. Gregory the Decapolite
04-18.text: Following thy master in asceticism, O John, thou didst become a vessel of grace; wherefore the Church honoureth thee together with the Decapolite.

04-19.title: Troparion of St. John of the Ancient Caves
04-19.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. John of the Ancient Caves, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

04-19.title: Kontakion of St. John of the Ancient Caves
04-19.text: Dwelling in the caves of Palestine, thou didst pour forth tears for thy soul, O John, and so didst find the bridechamber on high.

04-20.title: Troparion of St. Theodore Trichinas
