04-20.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Theodore Trichinas, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

04-20.title: Kontakion of St. Theodore Trichinas
04-20.text: Bearing only a hair-shirt upon thy flesh, thou didst trample upon the passions of the body, O Theodore; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee as a true ascetic.

04-21.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Januarius of Beneventum and his companions
04-21.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Januarius of Beneventum and his companions, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

04-21.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Januarius
04-21.text: With a sevenfold band of confessors thou didst seal thy faith in Beneventum, O Januarius; and thy relics pour forth blood ever-flowing as a sign of thy contests.

04-22.title: Troparion of St. Theodore the Sykeote
04-22.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Theodore the Sykeote, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

04-22.title: Kontakion of St. Theodore the Sykeote
04-22.text: Wonderworker and bishop of Anastasiopolis, thou didst shine forth in Galatia, O Theodore; and now from on high thou pourest forth grace upon them that flee unto thee.

04-23.title: Troparion of Great-martyr George
04-23.text: As the deliverer of captives and the protector of the poor, as the healer of the sick and the champion of kings, victorious great-martyr George, intercede with Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

04-23.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr George
04-23.text: Cultivated by God, thou didst appear as the husbandman of piety, gathering for thyself the sheaves of virtue; for thou didst sow with tears, but reapest with gladness, and in the contest didst pour forth thy blood, and didst receive Christ as thy crown.

04-24.title: Troparion of Martyr Sabbas the Stratelates and seventy soldiers with him
04-24.text: Thy Martyr Sabbas the Stratelates and seventy soldiers with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

04-24.title: Kontakion of Martyr Sabbas the Stratelates
04-24.text: Leading seventy soldiers unto the wreath of martyrdom, O Sabbas, thou didst exchange thy rank for the heavenly host; wherefore the Church doth crown thy noble band.

04-25.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist Mark
04-25.text: Thou wast the disciple and companion of the chief of the Apostles; in proclaiming the Gospel, thou didst illumine Egypt with the light of the knowledge of God. O Apostle and Evangelist Mark, intercede with Christ God to grant great mercy unto our souls.

04-25.title: Kontakion of Apostle and Evangelist Mark
04-25.text: Having received the grace of the Spirit on high from God, thou didst tear asunder the snares of the philosophers, O Apostle Mark; and having gathered all the nations, thou didst hand them over to thy Master.

04-26.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Basil
04-26.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Basil, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

04-26.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Basil of Amasea
04-26.text: Standing nobly before the tyrant, thou didst keep thy flock unstained, O Basil of Amasea, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the sword of martyrdom.

04-27.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Symeon
04-27.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Symeon, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

04-27.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Symeon, kinsman of the Lord
04-27.text: As a kinsman of Christ in the flesh and bishop of Jerusalem, thou didst tend His flock, O Symeon, and didst seal thy hierarchy upon the cross like the Master.

04-28.title: Troparion of Apostles Jason and Sosipater of the Seventy
04-28.text: O Holy Apostles Jason and Sosipater of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

04-28.title: Kontakion of Apostles Jason and Sosipater
04-28.text: Of the chorus of the seventy ye shone forth, O Jason and Sosipater, and didst preach Christ in Corfu, sealing your apostleship with the witness of blood.

04-29.title: Troparion of The Nine Martyrs of Cyzicus
04-29.text: Thy Martyr The Nine Martyrs of Cyzicus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

04-29.title: Kontakion of the Nine Martyrs of Cyzicus
04-29.text: As a ninefold chorus ye contended in Cyzicus, O holy martyrs, and didst pour forth a fragrance of confession upon the city; wherefore your relics granteth healing to the faithful.

04-30.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle James
04-30.text: O Holy Apostle James, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

04-30.title: Kontakion of Apostle James, brother of John
04-30.text: Hearing the voice divine that called thee, O Apostle James, and forsaking the love of thy father, thou didst hasten with John thy brother unto Christ, and with him didst attain the wreath of glory.

05-01.title: Troparion of Prophet Jeremiah
05-01.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Jeremiah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

05-01.title: Kontakion of Prophet Jeremiah
05-01.text: Cleansed in heart by the Spirit, O Jeremiah, thou didst become the seer of awesome mysteries, and didst foretell the new covenant; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

05-02.title: Troparion of St. Athanasius the Great
05-02.text: O thou who art a pillar of Orthodoxy, who didst confirm the Church through divine doctrines, hierarch Athanasius, thou hast been shown forth to be a sage of God; for by proclaiming the Son co-essential with the Father, thou didst put to shame the impious Arius. O righteous father, pray to Christ God to grant great mercy unto us.

05-02.title: Kontakion of St. Athanasius the Great
05-02.text: Planted in the hierarchy of Alexandria, O Athanasius, thou wast a pillar of Orthodoxy, confounding the heresy of Arius; wherefore the Church doth crown thee as Father of Fathers.

05-03.title: Troparion of the Venerable Theodosius of the Caves
05-03.text: Thou wast lifted up to the height of virtues, O Theodosius our father, and from thy youth didst love above all the monastic life; through patient labor thou didst attain it boldly, and didst become the dweller of the Caves of Kiev, adorning thy life with fasts and shining with the light of the Holy Spirit. Wherefore we cry to thee: rejoice, blessed father.

05-03.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Timothy and Maura
05-03.text: Joined together by Christian wedlock, O Timothy and Maura, ye were joined also in the wreath of martyrdom, hanging upon the cross of Christ in steadfast confession.

05-04.title: Troparion of Martyr Pelagia of Tarsus
05-04.text: Thy Martyr Pelagia of Tarsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-04.title: Kontakion of Martyr Pelagia
05-04.text: Having put away the world and its loves, thou didst run unto the Bridegroom Christ, O Pelagia of Tarsus, and within a brazen bull thou didst seal thy chastity.

05-05.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Irene of Macedonia
05-05.text: Thy Great-martyr Irene of Macedonia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-05.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Irene
05-05.text: From thy youth thou didst forsake the idols, O Irene, and didst convert thy parents unto Christ; and now thou prayest unceasingly for them that hymn thee.

05-06.title: Troparion of the Righteous Job
05-06.text: Hearing the goodness of Job, thou hast emulated his pious life. Living an upright life and pleasing God in works of mercy, thou didst become a wonderworker, righteous Job: thy mercies and almsgivings preserve us, by thine intercessions, before the Master.

05-06.title: Kontakion of Righteous Job the Long-suffering
05-06.text: As a true champion of patience thou didst endure the trials of the enemy, O Job, blessing the Lord both in plenty and in loss; wherefore we hymn thee as a pillar of patience.

05-07.title: Troparion of the Honorable Cross
05-07.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance, granting unto Thy people victory over all their enemies, and by the power of Thy Cross preserving Thy commonwealth.

05-07.title: Kontakion of the Sign of the Precious Cross at Jerusalem
05-07.text: On this day the heavens did proclaim the glory of the Cross above Jerusalem, more radiant than the sun; and we who behold the sign by faith cry out unto Thee: O Christ, save us by Thy Cross.

05-08.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist John
05-08.text: O Beloved Apostle of Christ our God, hasten to deliver thy defenseless people. He upon whose breast thou didst lean accepteth thee as thou kneelest before Him. Beseech Him, O Theologian, to scatter the persisting cloud of the heathen and ask for us peace and great mercy.

05-08.title: Kontakion of the Apostle and Evangelist John
05-08.text: Who can recount thy mighty works, O Virgin Apostle? For thou pourest forth miracles and effusions of healings, and prayest for our souls as Theologian and Friend of Christ.

05-09.title: Troparion of the Translation of the Relics of St. Nicholas
05-09.text: The day of bright festival is come; the city of Bari rejoiceth, and with her the whole world doth exult in hymns and psalms. For today is the sacred feast of the translation of the precious and much-healing relics of the hierarch Nicholas the wonderworker. Like a never-setting sun, having risen, he illumineth the faithful with the brilliance of his miracles.

05-09.title: Kontakion of the Translation of St. Nicholas to Bari
05-09.text: Lifted from the church of Myra by the providence of Christ, thy relics, O Nicholas, did light up the West; and the city of Bari doth rejoice at thy coming.

05-10.title: Troparion of Apostle Simon the Zealot
05-10.text: O Holy Apostle Simon the Zealot, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

05-10.title: Kontakion of Apostle Simon the Zealot
05-10.text: Like a zealot of the Spirit thou didst preach Christ unto the nations, O Simon, and didst seal thy apostleship with the witness of martyrdom; wherefore the Church doth hymn thy memory.

05-11.title: Troparion of Sts. Cyril and Methodius
05-11.text: O holy Equals-to-the-Apostles, illumined of the Slavic lands, fathers Cyril and Methodius wise in God: entreat the Master of all to confirm all Orthodox peoples in the true Faith and concord, granting peace unto the world and great mercy to our souls.

05-11.title: Kontakion of Sts. Cyril and Methodius
05-11.text: Equal-to-the-Apostles, ye translated the Scriptures into the Slavonic tongue, O Cyril and Methodius, and so didst enlighten whole nations; wherefore the Church of the Slavs blesseth your memory.

05-12.title: Troparion of St. Epiphanius of Cyprus
05-12.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Epiphanius of Cyprus, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

05-12.title: Kontakion of St. Epiphanius of Cyprus
05-12.text: Wonderworker of Cyprus and a great teacher of the Faith, thou didst confound the heresies with thy Panarion, O Epiphanius; wherefore we honour thee with hymns.

05-13.title: Troparion of Martyr Glyceria
05-13.text: Thy Martyr Glyceria, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-13.title: Kontakion of Martyr Glyceria
05-13.text: Having put off the corruptible vesture of the body, O Glyceria, thou didst clothe thyself in the imperishable robe of Christ; and so didst find the bridechamber on high.

05-14.title: Troparion of Martyr Isidore of Chios
05-14.text: Thy Martyr Isidore of Chios, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-14.title: Kontakion of Martyr Isidore of Chios
05-14.text: Forsaking the rank of the soldier, thou didst enlist in the army of Christ, O Isidore, and on the island of Chios didst seal thy faith with martyrdom.

05-15.title: Troparion of St. Pachomius the Great
05-15.text: Thou didst prove to be a citizen of the desert, an angel in the flesh, and a wonderworker, O Pachomius our God-bearing father. Having received gifts from heaven through fasting, vigil, and prayer, thou didst heal the sick and the souls of those who turned to thee in faith. Glory to Him who gave thee strength; glory to Him who crowned thee; glory to Him who through thee worketh healings for all.

05-15.title: Kontakion of St. Pachomius the Great
05-15.text: Having received the rule of cenobitic life from the Angel, O Pachomius, thou didst gather a multitude of monastics in the deserts of Tabennisi; wherefore we honour thee as a father of monastics.

05-16.title: Troparion of St. Theodore the Sanctified
05-16.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Theodore the Sanctified, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

05-16.title: Kontakion of St. Theodore the Sanctified
05-16.text: As a true disciple of Pachomius, thou didst inherit his blessing and continue his rule, O Theodore; and the Church doth honour thee as a sanctified father.

05-17.title: Troparion of Apostle Andronicus of the Seventy
05-17.text: O Holy Apostle Andronicus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

05-17.title: Kontakion of Apostle Andronicus
05-17.text: As a kinsman of Paul and his fellow-apostle, thou didst spread the Gospel among the nations, O Andronicus, and now thou prayest unceasingly for them that hymn thee.

05-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Theodotus of Ancyra and the Seven Virgins
05-18.text: Thy Martyr Theodotus of Ancyra and the Seven Virgins, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-18.title: Kontakion of Martyr Theodotus of Ancyra
05-18.text: Having raised up the bodies of the Seven Virgins from the lake, O Theodotus, thou didst follow them in martyrdom, and so didst find the unfading wreath.

05-19.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Patrick
05-19.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Patrick, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

05-19.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Patrick
05-19.text: Bishop of Prusa and confessor of the truth, thou didst stand before the tyrant unshaken, O Patrick, and didst seal thy faith with the steadfast witness of blood.

05-20.title: Troparion of Martyr Thalleleus
05-20.text: Thy Martyr Thalleleus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-20.title: Kontakion of Martyr Thalleleus
05-20.text: Like the youngest of the martyrs in years but a great one in soul, O Thalleleus, thou didst tread under foot the threats of the tyrant; wherefore the Church doth crown thee with hymns.

05-21.title: Troparion of Sts. Constantine and Helen
05-21.text: Having seen the image of Thy Cross in heaven, and like Paul having received the call not from men, Thine apostle among kings entrusted the imperial city to Thy hand. Save us always in peace, through the prayers of the Theotokos, O only Lover of mankind.

05-21.title: Kontakion of Sts. Constantine and Helen
05-21.text: Today Constantine and his mother Helen reveal the Cross, the all-precious wood. It is a shame to all the Jews, and a weapon of faithful kings against the adversary. For our sake hath appeared this mighty sign and awesome in war.

05-22.title: Troparion of Martyr Basiliscus
05-22.text: Thy Martyr Basiliscus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

05-22.title: Kontakion of Martyr Basiliscus
05-22.text: Bound in iron shoes, thou didst trample upon the snares of the deceiver, O Basiliscus, and didst pour forth healings as proof of thy contest.

05-23.title: Troparion of St. Michael the Confessor
05-23.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Michael the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

05-23.title: Kontakion of St. Michael of Synnada
05-23.text: Confessing the icons of Christ, thou didst endure exile, O Michael of Synnada, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the wreath of confession.

05-24.title: Troparion of St. Symeon the Stylite of the Wonderful Mountain
05-24.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Symeon the Stylite of the Wonderful Mountain, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

05-24.title: Kontakion of St. Symeon Stylite of the Wonderful Mountain
05-24.text: Standing upon a pillar from thy youth, O Symeon, thou didst gaze ever upon the heavens, and didst pour forth wonders upon Antioch from the Wonderful Mountain.

05-25.title: Troparion of the Third Finding of the Head of the Forerunner
05-25.text: The Forerunner's sacred head, having dawned forth from the earth, doth send forth incorrupt rays of healing to the faithful. From on high it gathereth the choir of angels, and from below it summoneth the company of mankind to send up with one voice glory unto Christ God.

05-25.title: Kontakion of the Third Finding of the Forerunner's Head
05-25.text: Thy head, O Forerunner of Christ, having been brought forth from the earth a third time, hath shone forth more brightly than the sun, illumining the faithful with the light of repentance.

05-26.title: Troparion of Apostle Carpus of the Seventy
05-26.text: O Holy Apostle Carpus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

05-26.title: Kontakion of Apostle Carpus
05-26.text: Companion of Paul and apostle of the seventy, thou didst preach Christ in Thrace, O Carpus; wherefore the Church doth hymn thy memory.

05-27.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Helladius
05-27.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Helladius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

05-27.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Helladius
05-27.text: As a true shepherd thou didst lay down thy life for the flock, O Helladius, and didst receive the wreath of martyrdom from the chief Shepherd Christ.

05-28.title: Troparion of St. Nicetas the Confessor
05-28.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Nicetas the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

05-28.title: Kontakion of St. Nicetas of Chalcedon
05-28.text: Confessing the icons of Christ in the second iconoclasm, O Nicetas of Chalcedon, thou didst endure exile; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee as a steadfast confessor.

05-29.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Tyre
05-29.text: Thy lamb Theodosia of Tyre, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

05-29.title: Kontakion of Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Tyre
05-29.text: At eighteen years of age thou didst run to the contest, O Theodosia, and being cast into the sea didst find the harbour of Christ; wherefore the Church doth crown thee with hymns.

05-30.title: Troparion of St. Isaac of Dalmatia
05-30.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Isaac of Dalmatia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

05-30.title: Kontakion of St. Isaac of Dalmatia
05-30.text: Standing nobly before the Emperor Valens, O Isaac, thou didst rebuke the Arian heresy and didst foretell his ruin; wherefore the Church doth bless thee as a confessor of Orthodoxy.

05-31.title: Troparion of Apostle Hermas of the Seventy
05-31.text: O Holy Apostle Hermas of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

05-31.title: Kontakion of Apostle Hermas
05-31.text: Of the chorus of the seventy thou didst shine forth, O Hermas, having written down the Shepherd from the angel's hand; wherefore the Church doth hymn thy memory.

06-01.title: Troparion of the Martyr Justin the Philosopher
06-01.text: Receiving godly wisdom through love of Christ and the radiance of the Holy Spirit, thou didst illumine the souls of the faithful with thy words and didst shame the demons of heathen sophistry. Pray to Christ God for our souls, O philosopher Justin.

06-01.title: Kontakion of Martyr Justin the Philosopher
06-01.text: Having sought the truth among the philosophers, O Justin, thou didst find Christ as the true Philosophy, and didst seal thy confession with thy blood in Rome.

06-02.title: Troparion of St. Nicephorus the Confessor
06-02.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Nicephorus the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-02.title: Kontakion of St. Nicephorus the Confessor
06-02.text: Patriarch of Constantinople and confessor of the icons, O Nicephorus, thou didst endure exile and reproach, and didst seal thy hierarchy with confession.

06-03.title: Troparion of Martyr Lucillian and those with him
06-03.text: Thy Martyr Lucillian and those with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-03.title: Kontakion of Martyr Lucillian and Companions
06-03.text: Once a pagan priest in old age, thou didst find Christ, O Lucillian, and didst lead four young martyrs with thee unto the heavenly bridechamber.

06-04.title: Troparion of St. Metrophanes
06-04.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Metrophanes, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-04.title: Kontakion of St. Metrophanes of Constantinople
06-04.text: First patriarch of the New Rome, O Metrophanes, thou didst bless the holy Council of Nicaea, and didst plant Orthodoxy upon the imperial city.

06-05.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Dorotheus
06-05.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Dorotheus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-05.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Dorotheus of Tyre
06-05.text: Bishop of Tyre and confessor of Christ, thou didst write down the witness of the apostles, O Dorotheus, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the wreath of martyrdom.

06-06.title: Troparion of St. Hilarion the New of Dalmatia
06-06.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Hilarion the New of Dalmatia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-06.title: Kontakion of St. Hilarion the New of Dalmatia
06-06.text: Continuing the labours of the elder Hilarion, thou didst defend the icons of Christ, O father, and didst seal thy abbacy with confession.

06-07.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Theodotus of Ancyra
06-07.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Theodotus of Ancyra, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-07.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Theodotus of Ancyra
06-07.text: As an inn-keeper of Ancyra who lodged the saints, O Theodotus, thou didst follow them unto the wreath of martyrdom; wherefore the Church doth crown thy memory.

06-08.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro (Stratelates)
06-08.text: Thy Great-martyr Translation of the Relics of Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro (Stratelates), O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-08.title: Kontakion of the Translation of Theodore the Stratelates
06-08.text: Returned to thy native city of Euchaita, thy relics shine forth as a fountain of healings, O Theodore the General, and the Church doth crown thy noble contest.

06-09.title: Troparion of St. Cyril
06-09.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Cyril, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-09.title: Kontakion of St. Cyril of Alexandria
06-09.text: Like a pillar of fire shining in Alexandria, thou didst confound the impiety of Nestorius, O Cyril, and didst confirm the Theotokos in her divine motherhood; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee as champion of the Mother of God.

06-10.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Timothy
06-10.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Timothy, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-10.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Timothy of Prussa
06-10.text: Wonderworker and bishop of Prussa, thou didst destroy the dragon by prayer, O Timothy, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the witness of martyrdom.

06-11.title: Troparion of Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas
06-11.text: O Holy Apostle Bartholomew and Barnabas, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

06-11.title: Kontakion of Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas
06-11.text: As a holy pair of apostles ye shone forth, preaching Christ unto the nations, O Bartholomew and Barnabas; and ye sealed your witness with martyrdom.

06-12.title: Troparion of St. Onuphrius the Great
06-12.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Onuphrius the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-12.title: Kontakion of St. Onuphrius the Great
06-12.text: In the wilderness for seventy years thou didst contend, O Onuphrius, clothed only with thine own hair and fed by an angel; wherefore we hymn thee as a wonder among ascetics.

06-13.title: Troparion of Martyr Aquilina of Byblos
06-13.text: Thy Martyr Aquilina of Byblos, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-13.title: Kontakion of Martyr Aquilina
06-13.text: Like a young lamb among wolves thou didst contend at Byblos, O Aquilina, and didst pour forth thy blood as a libation unto the Master.

06-14.title: Troparion of the Prophet Elisha
06-14.text: O great Prophet Elisha, thou didst receive a double portion of the spirit of Elijah, and the gift of healing all sicknesses and infirmities of body and soul. Pray now to the Lord that mercy may be granted to our souls.

06-14.title: Kontakion of Prophet Elisha
06-14.text: Heir of the spirit of Elias by a twofold portion, O Elisha, thou didst pour forth wonders among Israel; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a great prophet.

06-15.title: Troparion of Prophet Amos
06-15.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Amos, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

06-15.title: Kontakion of Prophet Amos
06-15.text: Cleansing the threshing-floor of God by thy preaching, O Amos, thou didst proclaim the Day of the Lord; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

06-16.title: Troparion of St. Tikhon the Wonderworker
06-16.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Tikhon the Wonderworker, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-16.title: Kontakion of St. Tikhon of Amathus
06-16.text: Wonderworker and bishop of Amathus in Cyprus, O Tikhon, thou didst command the dead vine to bear fruit; wherefore the faithful flee unto thee for healing.

06-17.title: Troparion of Martyrs Manuel
06-17.text: Thy Martyrs Manuel, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-17.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Manuel, Sabel, and Ishmael
06-17.text: Three Persian brothers in the flesh and in martyrdom, O Manuel, Sabel, and Ishmael, ye refused the worship of the sun and didst confess Christ; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble band.

06-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Leontius
06-18.text: Thy Martyr Leontius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-18.title: Kontakion of Martyr Leontius
06-18.text: Soldier of the Roman emperor and athlete of the heavenly King, thou didst contend nobly at Tripoli, O Leontius, and didst attain the wreath of victory.

06-19.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Jude
06-19.text: O Holy Apostle Jude, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

06-19.title: Kontakion of Apostle Jude
06-19.text: Brother of the Lord according to the flesh and a true apostle of Christ, O Jude, thou didst pour forth thy Epistle as a defense of the Faith; wherefore the Church doth hymn thy memory.

06-20.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Methodius
06-20.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Methodius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-20.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Methodius of Patara
06-20.text: Confounding the godless Origen and his disciples, O Methodius of Patara, thou didst seal thy hierarchy with the witness of martyrdom; wherefore the Church doth bless thy memory.

06-21.title: Troparion of Martyr Julian of Tarsus
06-21.text: Thy Martyr Julian of Tarsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-21.title: Kontakion of Martyr Julian of Tarsus
06-21.text: Cast into a sack of vipers by the tyrant, O Julian, thou didst find Christ as the Comforter; and so didst earn the wreath of unfading glory.

06-22.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Eusebius
06-22.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Eusebius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-22.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Eusebius of Samosata
06-22.text: Champion of Orthodoxy and friend of Basil the Great, O Eusebius, thou didst endure exile from thy see, and at the hands of a heretical woman thou didst attain the wreath of martyrdom.

06-23.title: Troparion of Martyr Agrippina of Rome
06-23.text: Thy Martyr Agrippina of Rome, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-23.title: Kontakion of Martyr Agrippina of Rome
06-23.text: Once a senatorial maiden of Rome, thou didst forsake the world for Christ, O Agrippina, and didst seal thy confession with martyrdom in the days of Valerian.

06-24.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Forerunner
06-24.text: O Prophet and Forerunner of the coming of Christ, though we honor thee with love, we cannot worthily extol thee, for thy mother's barrenness and thy father's voicelessness were undone by thy honored and glorious nativity, and the Incarnation of the Son of God was proclaimed to the world.

06-24.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of the Forerunner
06-24.text: She who was once barren beareth today the Forerunner of Christ, who is the fulfilment of every prophecy; for he, the prophet, in the Jordan laid his hand upon Him whom the prophets foretold, and was revealed as the Forerunner, the messenger, and prophet of the Word.

06-25.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Febronia of Nisibis
06-25.text: Thy lamb Febronia of Nisibis, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

06-25.title: Kontakion of Virgin-martyr Febronia
06-25.text: Having forsaken the love of this fleeting world, thou didst run unto the Bridegroom Christ, O Febronia, and within the convent of Nisibis didst pour forth thy blood for the Faith.

06-26.title: Troparion of St. David of Thessalonica
06-26.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. David of Thessalonica, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-26.title: Kontakion of St. David of Thessalonica
06-26.text: From a tree in Thessalonica thou didst preach to the people, O David, and didst pour forth wonders by the Spirit; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a venerable father.

06-27.title: Troparion of St. Sampson the Hospitable
06-27.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Sampson the Hospitable, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-27.title: Kontakion of St. Sampson the Hospitable
06-27.text: As a healer of bodies and a father of strangers, O Sampson, thou didst build a great hospital in Constantinople, and pourest forth thereunto, even now, the streams of healing.

06-28.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of the Unmercenaries Cyrus and John
06-28.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Translation of the Relics of the Unmercenaries Cyrus and John, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-28.title: Kontakion of the Translation of Cyrus and John
06-28.text: Brought from Egypt unto Rome, your honoured relics, O Cyrus and John, did glorify the imperial city; and now from on high ye bestow healing without payment.

06-29.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
06-29.text: O first-throned of the Apostles and teachers of the world, intercede with the Master of all that He may grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

06-29.title: Kontakion of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
06-29.text: O Lord, Thou hast received the steadfast and divinely-inspired heralds, the chief among Thine Apostles, into the enjoyment of Thy good things and into Thy rest, for Thou hast accepted their labors and their death as above every burnt offering, O Thou who alone knowest the hearts of men.

06-30.title: Troparion of Synaxis of the Holy
06-30.text: O Holy Apostle Synaxis of the Holy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

06-30.title: Kontakion of the Synaxis of the Twelve Apostles
06-30.text: Christ the Rock most gloriously doth glorify the rock of faith, the chief of the disciples; and He summoneth all to praise the chorus of the twelve, the foundation-stones of the Church, which the gates of hell shall not prevail against.

07-01.title: Troparion of Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Rome
07-01.text: O holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Rome, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

07-01.title: Kontakion of Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Rome
07-01.text: Having received the grace of healings, ye bestow healing without payment upon them that flee unto you, O glorious Cosmas and Damian; and pourest forth the streams of mercy upon all the faithful.

07-02.title: Troparion of St. John of Shanghai and San Francisco
07-02.text: Lo, thy care for thy flock in its sojourn hath prefigured the supplications which thou dost ever offer up for the whole world. Thus do we believe, having come to know thy love, O Holy Hierarch John the Wonderworker. Wholly sanctified by God through the ministry of the all-pure Mysteries, and thyself ever strengthened thereby, thou didst hasten to the suffering, O most gladsome healer. Hasten now also to the aid of us who honor thee with all our heart.

07-02.title: Kontakion of the Placing of the Robe of the Theotokos
07-02.text: Like a precious robe of immortality, thy garment is laid in Blachernae, O Theotokos, as a protection unto the imperial city; and we hymn it as a defense and wall.

07-03.title: Troparion of Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea in Cappadocia
07-03.text: Thy Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea in Cappadocia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-03.title: Kontakion of Martyr Hyacinth
07-03.text: Once a young chamberlain of the Emperor Trajan, thou didst refuse the meats of the idols, O Hyacinth, and didst seal thy faith by famine; wherefore the Church doth crown thee with hymns.

07-04.title: Troparion of St. Andrew of Crete
07-04.text: By thy holy life thou didst shine forth as a star in the firmament of the Church, O Father Andrew; thou didst pour forth like a fountain the sweetness of psalmody, and didst nourish the faithful through hymns of penitence. Pray to Christ God to save our souls.

07-04.title: Kontakion of St. Andrew of Crete
07-04.text: Thou didst pour forth canons of contrition, O Andrew of Crete, and thy Great Canon doth stir the faithful unto repentance; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

07-05.title: Troparion of St. Athanasius of Mount Athos
07-05.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Athanasius of Mount Athos, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

07-05.title: Kontakion of St. Athanasius of Athos
07-05.text: Father of the Great Lavra of the Holy Mountain, O Athanasius, thou didst gather a multitude of monastics on Athos, and didst plant therein the cenobitic rule.

07-06.title: Troparion of St. Sisoes the Great
07-06.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Sisoes the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

07-06.title: Kontakion of St. Sisoes the Great
07-06.text: Dwelling in the cell of Anthony the Great, thou didst inherit his graces, O Sisoes, and didst weep even at thy departure: 'Have I yet begun repentance?' Wherefore the Church doth honour thee.

07-07.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Kyriake of Nicomedia
07-07.text: Thy Great-martyr Kyriake of Nicomedia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-07.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Kyriake
07-07.text: Lover of the Lord's day from thy youth, O Kyriake, thou didst dedicate thy virginity unto Christ, and didst pour forth thy blood in steadfast confession.

07-08.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Procopius of Caesarea in Palestine
07-08.text: Thy Great-martyr Procopius of Caesarea in Palestine, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-08.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Procopius
07-08.text: Once a persecutor of the Faith, thou wast called by Christ as another Paul, O Procopius, and didst seal thy zeal with the blood of martyrdom; wherefore the Church doth glorify thee.

07-09.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Pancratius
07-09.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Pancratius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

07-09.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Pancratius
07-09.text: Disciple of the Apostle Peter and bishop of Tauromenium, O Pancratius, thou didst water Sicily with the streams of preaching, and didst seal thy hierarchy with martyrdom.

07-10.title: Troparion of the Venerable Anthony of the Caves
07-10.text: Having departed from the tumults of the world, with faith thou didst leave thy fatherland, and didst follow Christ, settling in the holy mountain of Athos; and thence, by the command of the Mother of God, thou didst come to the mountains of Kiev. There thou didst end thy life in fastings, fathering many monks who came to thee. Wherefore, having received from God the gift of wonders, O Venerable Anthony, pray for our souls.

07-10.title: Kontakion of the Forty-Five Martyrs of Nicopolis
07-10.text: Bound together in chains and cast into the fire, ye were not consumed, O forty-five soldiers of Christ; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble band with hymns.

07-11.title: Troparion of St. Olga of Kiev, Equal-to-the-Apostles
07-11.text: Giving wings of mind to the knowledge of God, thou hast spread thy great desires to look upon Christ. Thou hast also led thy beloved grandson Vladimir along the path which thou hast trod. Wherefore, O Olga, equal-to-the-apostles, we celebrate thy honored memory before Christ God, asking forgiveness of our sins.

07-11.title: Kontakion of Equal-to-the-Apostles Olga
07-11.text: Grandmother of the Russian land and herald of the Faith, O Olga, thou didst forsake the gods of thy people and didst embrace Christ; wherefore the Church doth bless thee as enlightener.

07-12.title: Troparion of the Venerable Paisios the Athonite
07-12.text: The dweller of Mount Athos and the boast of Cappadocia, the new gift of God to the faithful — let us honor him with hymns, O lovers of the saints; for the Paraclete enriched him with the multitude of His gifts, and thou hast become a shelter for all who hasten to thee, O blessed Paisios, our Holy Father.

07-12.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Proclus and Hilary
07-12.text: Joined together by the bond of love, ye contended for the Faith, O Proclus and Hilary, and ye received together the wreaths of martyrdom from Christ.

07-13.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
07-13.text: Captains of the heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy beseech thee that by your supplications thou wilt encompass us beneath the wings of thy immaterial glory, and faithfully preserve us who fall down and cry to thee: deliver us from all dangers, for ye are the commanders of the powers on high.

07-13.title: Kontakion of the Synaxis of the Archangel Gabriel
07-13.text: Captain of the bodiless powers, thou didst proclaim unto the Virgin the descent of the Word, O Gabriel; wherefore the Church doth honour thy synaxis with hymns.

07-14.title: Troparion of Apostle Aquila of the Seventy
07-14.text: O Holy Apostle Aquila of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-14.title: Kontakion of Apostle Aquila
07-14.text: Companion of Paul and a tent-maker of Christ's mysteries, O Aquila, thou with thy wife Priscilla didst pour forth the seeds of preaching.

07-15.title: Troparion of St. Vladimir of Kiev, Equal-to-the-Apostles
07-15.text: Thou wast like a merchant seeking a goodly pearl, O sovereign Vladimir, sitting upon the high throne of the mother of the cities of God, the God-saved Kiev. For having tested and sent forth thine envoys to the Imperial City to behold the Orthodox Faith, thou didst find the priceless pearl, even Christ, who from eternity chose thee as another Paul, and shook off all blindness, both spiritual and bodily, in the holy font. Therefore, we thy children celebrate thy repose: entreat Christ to save the Russian land and to grant our souls great mercy.

07-15.title: Kontakion of Equal-to-the-Apostles Vladimir
07-15.text: Once a pagan prince of the Russian land, thou wast called by Christ as another Paul, O Vladimir, and didst baptize Russia in the streams of the Dnieper; wherefore the Church blesseth thee.

07-16.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Athenogenes
07-16.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Athenogenes, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

07-16.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Athenogenes
07-16.text: As a noble priest of God, thou didst seal thy hierarchy with the blood of martyrdom, O Athenogenes of Heracleopolis, having entered the flames as into a fragrant garden.

07-17.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Marina
07-17.text: Thy lamb Marina, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking thee I contest. I am crucified with thee, and in thy baptism am buried with thee. I suffer for thy sake, that I might reign with thee; I die for thee, that I might live in thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to thee in love. By her prayers save our souls, O thou who art merciful.

07-17.title: Troparion of the Royal Martyrs of Russia
07-17.text: Most-noble and right-believing passion-bearers, gentle Tsar Nicholas and his most-pious consort Alexandra, with their God-beloved children Olga, Tatiana, Maria, Anastasia, and the Tsarevich Alexei: ye were martyred for Christ by the godless, the icons of every Russian Christian household to come. Pray to the merciful Master for our country, that we may walk in His paths and find rest with you in His mercy.

07-17.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Marina
07-17.text: Adorned with the beauty of virginity, O Marina, thou didst tread upon the dragon by the sign of the Cross, and didst pour forth thy blood as a sacrifice unto Christ.

07-18.title: Troparion of the New Martyr Grand Duchess Elizabeth
07-18.text: Emulating the Lord's self-abasement on the earth, O Grand Duchess Elizabeth, thou didst give up the comforts of palaces to dwell in the slums, console the suffering, and labor as a sister of mercy among the poor; entering thus into the joy of the bridegroom Christ, with the sister Varvara thou wast crowned with martyrdom by the godless. With them pray to the lover of mankind, that He save our souls.

07-18.title: Kontakion of Martyr Emilian
07-18.text: Once a slave of Silistria according to the flesh, thou wast made free in Christ, O Emilian, and didst seal thy confession by the fire of martyrdom.

07-19.title: Troparion of St. Macrina
07-19.text: In thee, O Mother, was carefully preserved what was according to the image; for taking up thy cross, thou didst follow Christ. By so doing, thou didst teach us to disregard the flesh, for it passeth away, but to care for the soul as a thing immortal. Wherefore, O righteous Macrina, thy spirit rejoiceth with the angels.

07-19.title: Kontakion of St. Macrina
07-19.text: Sister of Basil and Gregory according to the flesh, and an elder mother of monastics, O Macrina, thou didst teach the household of God by thy life.

07-20.title: Troparion of the Prophet Elijah
07-20.text: O angelic incarnate prophet, foundation of prophets, the second forerunner of the coming of Christ, glorious Elijah, who from on high didst send down grace upon Elisha to dispel diseases and cleanse lepers, for those who honor thee thou pourest forth abundant healings.

07-20.title: Kontakion of Prophet Elijah
07-20.text: Prophet and seer of the works of God, O Elijah great in renown, thou didst shut up the rain-giving clouds by thy word; pray on our behalf unto the only Lover of mankind.

07-21.title: Troparion of Sts. Symeon the Fool-for-Christ and John
07-21.text: O holy Sts. Symeon the Fool-for-Christ and John, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

07-21.title: Kontakion of Sts. Symeon the Fool and John
07-21.text: Having put on the cloak of folly for Christ, O Symeon, thou didst rebuke the world by thy seeming madness; and with thee we honour John the hermit, who in the wilderness did pray.

07-22.title: Troparion of St. Mary Magdalene
07-22.text: Christ, who was born of the Virgin for our sake, did Thou deliver from Whom didst expel seven demons, holy Mary Magdalene. Thou didst become the first to behold the divine Resurrection, and as the equal-to-the-Apostles, thou didst preach Him risen from the dead unto all the ends of the earth.

07-22.title: Kontakion of Mary Magdalene
07-22.text: From thee did the Lord cast out seven demons, O Mary Magdalene, and made thee a herald of His Resurrection; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as Equal-to-the-Apostles.

07-23.title: Troparion of Martyrs Trophimus
07-23.text: Thy Martyrs Trophimus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-23.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Trophimus and Companions
07-23.text: Joined together by the bond of love, ye contended unto the wreaths of martyrdom, O Trophimus, Theophilus, and ye thirteen who with them confessed Christ.

07-24.title: Troparion of the Holy Passion-Bearers Boris and Gleb
07-24.text: True passion-bearers and observers of the gospel commandments, blessed Boris and Gleb, you did not resist the attacks of your brother, your enemy; he killed your bodies, yet was unable to touch your souls. Therefore let evil rulers tremble, while the choirs of angels rejoice, praising the pair who pleased God. Together, intercede that our country may be saved and our souls find great mercy.

07-24.title: Kontakion of Passion-Bearers Boris and Gleb
07-24.text: Once princes of Russia in the flesh, ye refused to lift the sword against your brother, O Boris and Gleb, and so received the wreath of meek passion-bearing.

07-25.title: Troparion of the Dormition of Saint Anna
07-25.text: O Foremother of Christ, we entreat thee to send down the favors of thy daughter, the Theotokos, upon those who hymn thy memory in faith. Thou hast brought into the world the holy Mother of God who bore the eternal life. Wherefore, with joy in thy dormition thou hast departed to the Lord. Pray for those who keep thy memory.

07-25.title: Kontakion of the Dormition of Righteous Anna
07-25.text: Mother of the Mother of God, righteous Anna, thou didst bear the burden of barrenness with patience, and at last didst bring forth the dwelling-place of God; wherefore we hymn thy dormition.

07-26.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Hermolaus and those with him
07-26.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Hermolaus and those with him, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

07-26.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Hermolaus
07-26.text: Catechist of the holy Panteleimon, O Hermolaus, thou didst share with him the wreath of martyrdom; and we honour thy teaching and thy contest.

07-27.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Panteleimon
07-27.text: O holy prize-winner and healer Panteleimon, intercede with our merciful God to grant our souls forgiveness of sins.

07-27.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Panteleimon
07-27.text: Imitator of the merciful one and recipient of grace from Him, O glorious passion-bearer Panteleimon, healer of soldiers, intercede with the only Lover of mankind, that He may grant unto our souls great mercy.

07-28.title: Troparion of Holy Apostles and Deacons Prochorus
07-28.text: O Holy Apostle and Deacons Prochorus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-28.title: Kontakion of Apostles Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, and Parmenas
07-28.text: As a fourfold band of the seven deacons ordained by the apostles, ye preached Christ unto the nations, O Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, and Parmenas; wherefore the Church doth hymn your memory.

07-29.title: Troparion of Martyr Callinicus of Cilicia
07-29.text: Thy Martyr Callinicus of Cilicia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-29.title: Kontakion of Martyr Callinicus
07-29.text: Once a soldier of Cilicia, thou didst forsake the world for Christ, O Callinicus, and didst attain the wreath of martyrdom by iron shoes pressed unto thy feet.

07-30.title: Troparion of Apostles Silas
07-30.text: O Holy Apostles Silas, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-30.title: Kontakion of Apostles Silas, Silvanus, and Companions
07-30.text: Companions of Paul and apostles of the seventy, ye scattered the seeds of preaching, O Silas, Silvanus, Crescens, Epenetus, and Andronicus; wherefore the Church doth honour your memory.

07-31.title: Troparion of Righteous Eudokimos of Cappadocia
07-31.text: Great are the achievements of faith! O Righteous Righteous Eudokimos of Cappadocia, thou didst shine like a star upon the earth, leaving us a model of holy life. Through thy prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

07-31.title: Kontakion of Righteous Eudokimos
07-31.text: Once a senatorial youth of Cappadocia, thou didst conceal thy holiness from men, O Eudokimos, and at thy youthful repose the Lord did glorify thee with wonders.

08-01.title: Troparion of the Honorable Cross
08-01.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance, granting unto Thy people victory over all their enemies, and by the power of Thy Cross preserving Thy commonwealth.

08-01.title: Kontakion of the Procession of the Cross and the Maccabee Martyrs
08-01.text: Brought forth from the imperial treasury, the Cross is set forth as a fountain of healings; and with it we honour the seven Maccabee Martyrs, their mother Solomonia, and their teacher Eleazar.

08-02.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of the Protomartyr and Archdeacon Stephen
08-02.text: Thy Martyr Translation of the Relics of the Protomartyr and Archdeacon Stephen, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-02.title: Kontakion of the Translation of Protomartyr Stephen
08-02.text: Discovered to his patriarch by a wondrous vision, thy honoured relics, O Stephen, were translated unto the imperial city, and now do shine forth as the dawn of healings.

08-03.title: Troparion of St. Anthony the Roman
08-03.text: O Wonderworker Anthony the Roman, thou wast filled with grace from on high and didst pour out unto the faithful the gift of healing. Pray Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

08-03.title: Kontakion of St. Anthony the Roman
08-03.text: Borne by the sea from Rome unto Novgorod upon a stone, O Anthony, thou didst found there a monastery, and didst pour forth a fountain of teaching to a strange people.

08-04.title: Troparion of The Seven Holy Youths of Ephesus
08-04.text: O Holy Apostle The Seven Holy Youths of Ephesus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-04.title: Kontakion of the Seven Holy Youths of Ephesus
08-04.text: Asleep within the cave of Ephesus for two centuries, ye awoke as proof of the Resurrection, O seven holy youths; wherefore the Church doth bless your wondrous repose.

08-05.title: Troparion of Forefeast of the Holy Transfiguration; Martyr Eusignius
08-05.text: Thy Martyr Forefeast of the Holy Transfiguration; Martyr Eusignius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-05.title: Kontakion of the Forefeast of the Transfiguration
08-05.text: Today the Father's voice prepareth to proclaim on Tabor: This is My beloved Son; and the Spirit prepareth to overshadow the mountain with the cloud of glory.

08-06.title: Troparion of the Transfiguration
08-06.text: Thou wast transfigured on the mount, O Christ God, revealing Thy glory to Thy disciples as far as they could bear it. Let Thine everlasting light shine also upon us sinners, through the prayers of the Theotokos. O Giver of light, glory to Thee.

08-06.title: Kontakion of the Transfiguration
08-06.text: On the mountain wast Thou transfigured, O Christ God, and Thy disciples beheld Thy glory as far as they could bear it, so that when they would behold Thee crucified they would understand Thy voluntary Passion, and proclaim to the world that Thou art truly the radiance of the Father.

08-07.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Dometius the Persian
08-07.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Dometius the Persian, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

08-07.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Dometius the Persian
08-07.text: Once a Persian by birth, thou didst forsake the worship of fire for the worship of Christ, O Dometius, and within a cave in Syria didst seal thy confession by martyrdom.

08-08.title: Troparion of St. Emilian the Confessor
08-08.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Emilian the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

08-08.title: Kontakion of St. Emilian of Cyzicus
08-08.text: Confessing the icons of Christ before the iconoclasts, O Emilian of Cyzicus, thou didst endure exile from thy see, and didst depart unto the heavenly Jerusalem.

08-09.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Matthias
08-09.text: O Holy Apostle Matthias, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-09.title: Kontakion of Apostle Matthias
08-09.text: Chosen by lot to fill the place of the betrayer, O Matthias, thou didst preach Christ unto the nations, and didst seal thy apostleship with the witness of martyrdom.

08-10.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr and Archdeacon Lawrence of Rome
08-10.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr and Archdeacon Lawrence of Rome, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

08-10.title: Kontakion of Archdeacon Lawrence
08-10.text: Bestowing upon the poor the treasures of the Church of Rome, O Lawrence, thou didst lay them up in heaven, and upon a gridiron didst seal thy diaconate with the witness of fire.

08-11.title: Troparion of Martyr Euplus the Deacon of Catania
08-11.text: Thy Martyr Euplus the Deacon of Catania, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-11.title: Kontakion of Martyr Euplus the Deacon
08-11.text: Bearing the Gospel-book at thy breast, O Euplus, thou didst preach Christ openly at Catania, and didst seal thy diaconate with the sword of martyrdom.

08-12.title: Troparion of Martyrs Photius and Anicetus of Nicomedia
08-12.text: Thy Martyrs Photius and Anicetus of Nicomedia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-12.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Photius and Anicetus
08-12.text: Yoked together by the bond of love, ye contended unto the wreaths of martyrdom, O Photius and Anicetus, refusing to bow down before the idols of Nicomedia.

08-13.title: Troparion of St. Tikhon
08-13.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Tikhon, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

08-13.title: Kontakion of St. Tikhon of Zadonsk
08-13.text: Patriarch of teaching and example of meekness, O Tikhon of Zadonsk, thou didst forsake the see of Voronezh to dwell in stillness; and from thy writings the faithful drink the streams of repentance.

08-14.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
08-14.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

08-14.title: Kontakion of the Forefeast of the Dormition and Prophet Micah
08-14.text: Today the powers of heaven prepare to receive the Theotokos, and with them we honour the Prophet Micah, who foretold the One who would come forth from Bethlehem of Judea.

08-15.title: Troparion of the Dormition
08-15.text: In giving birth thou didst preserve thy virginity; in thy dormition thou didst not forsake the world, O Theotokos. Thou wast translated unto life, since thou art the Mother of Life, and by thine intercessions thou dost deliver our souls from death.

08-15.title: Kontakion of the Dormition
08-15.text: Neither the tomb nor death had power over the Theotokos, who is sleepless in her intercessions and unfailing hope in her protection; for as the Mother of Life she hath been translated unto life by Him who dwelt in her ever-virgin womb.

08-16.title: Troparion of the Feast
08-16.text: Translation of the Image of Christ Not-Made-by-Hands from Edessa — let us celebrate the great mystery of our salvation, the kindness of God in His coming, the love of God for the world.

08-16.title: Kontakion of the Image of Christ Not-Made-by-Hands
08-16.text: Receiving the holy image upon a cloth, King Abgar of Edessa was healed by it; and now from Edessa unto Constantinople is this image borne, granting blessing unto the city.

08-17.title: Troparion of Martyr Myron of Cyzicus
08-17.text: Thy Martyr Myron of Cyzicus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-17.title: Kontakion of Martyr Myron of Cyzicus
08-17.text: Once a priest of Cyzicus, thou didst stand for Christ before the persecutor, O Myron, and within an iron skin didst pour forth thy blood for the Faith.

08-18.title: Troparion of Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria
08-18.text: Thy Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-18.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Florus and Laurus
08-18.text: Brothers in the flesh and brothers in the wreath of martyrdom, O Florus and Laurus, ye preached Christ unto the masons of Illyria, and didst seal your faith in a well.

08-19.title: Troparion of Martyr Andrew Stratelates and 2593 with him
08-19.text: Thy Martyr Andrew Stratelates and 2593 with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-19.title: Kontakion of Martyr Andrew Stratelates
08-19.text: Like another general of Christ thou didst lead a great army unto the heavenly host, O Andrew the Stratelates; and with thy soldiers didst attain the wreath of victory.

08-20.title: Troparion of the Prophet Samuel
08-20.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Samuel, O Lord: through him we beseech thee, save our souls.

08-20.title: Kontakion of Prophet Samuel
08-20.text: From thy mother's vow thou wast given to the Lord, O Samuel, and didst anoint the kings of Israel; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a great prophet.

08-21.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Thaddaeus of the Seventy
08-21.text: O Holy Apostle Thaddaeus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-21.title: Kontakion of Apostle Thaddaeus
08-21.text: Of the seventy disciples thou wast a herald, O Thaddaeus, and didst bring the holy image of Christ unto King Abgar of Edessa; wherefore the Church doth bless thy memory.

08-22.title: Troparion of Martyr Agathonicus of Nicomedia
08-22.text: Thy Martyr Agathonicus of Nicomedia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-22.title: Kontakion of Martyr Agathonicus
08-22.text: Like a noble champion of the Faith, O Agathonicus, thou didst stand before the tyrant Maximian, and within a wagon yoked to oxen didst seal thy confession by martyrdom.

08-23.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
08-23.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

08-23.title: Kontakion of the Leavetaking of the Dormition and Irenaeus of Lyons
08-23.text: Today the Church doth bless the Dormition of the Theotokos, and with it we honour Irenaeus of Lyons, foe of the Gnostics and shepherd of Gaul, who sealed his hierarchy by martyrdom.

08-24.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Eutyches
08-24.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Eutyches, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

08-24.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Eutyches
08-24.text: Disciple of John the Theologian, O Eutyches, thou didst preach Christ in Phrygia, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the wreath of martyrdom.

08-25.title: Troparion of Return of the Relics of the Apostle Bartholomew; Apostle Titus of the Seventy
08-25.text: O Holy Apostle Return of the Relics of the Apostle Bartholomew; Apostle Titus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-25.title: Kontakion of the Return of Bartholomew and Apostle Titus
08-25.text: Returning to the East from the islands, the relics of Bartholomew the apostle do shine forth; and with him we honour Titus the apostle, the disciple of Paul.

08-26.title: Troparion of Martyrs Adrian and Natalia
08-26.text: Thy Martyrs Adrian and Natalia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-26.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Adrian and Natalia
08-26.text: Once a persecutor of the Faith, O Adrian, thou wast won by the steadfastness of the martyrs, and didst find in Natalia thy wife a true bride for the heavenly bridechamber.

08-27.title: Troparion of St. Poemen the Great
08-27.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Poemen the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

08-27.title: Kontakion of St. Poemen the Great
08-27.text: A shepherd of monastics by name and a true shepherd by deed, O Poemen, thou didst gather a multitude in Egypt, and didst pour forth sayings of life unto thy disciples.

08-28.title: Troparion of the Venerable Moses the Black
08-28.text: Thou didst forsake Egypt's wickedness and didst draw nigh unto God, O Venerable Father Moses; thou didst earnestly take up the cross of asceticism, and didst become a wonderworker, the shepherd of lions and of the souls who came to thee. Pray for those who keep thy memory, that Christ may grant us His great mercy.

08-28.title: Kontakion of St. Moses the Black
08-28.text: Once a robber and a slave of vice, thou didst find Christ in the wilderness of Scetis, O Moses, and didst water it with thy tears, becoming a vessel of grace.

08-29.title: Troparion of the Beheading of the Forerunner
08-29.text: The just man is remembered with songs of praise, but for thee the testimony of the Lord is sufficient, O Forerunner. Truly thou wast revealed more honorable than the prophets, for thou wast deemed worthy to baptize in the Jordan Him whom they had preached. Therefore having contested for the truth, thou didst rejoice to bring the good tidings even to those in hell — that God hath appeared in the flesh, taking away the sin of the world and granting us great mercy.

08-29.title: Kontakion of the Beheading of the Forerunner
08-29.text: The glorious beheading of the Forerunner became an act of divine dispensation, that he might preach the coming of the Saviour even unto those in Hades. Wherefore let Herodias lament, who solicited a wicked murder, having loved not the law of God or eternal life, but the deceitful and temporal.

08-30.title: Troparion of Sts. Alexander
08-30.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Sts. Alexander, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

08-30.title: Kontakion of Sts. Alexander, John, and Paul of Constantinople
08-30.text: As a threefold band of hierarchs ye shepherded the New Rome, O Alexander, John, and Paul, and ye stood unshaken against the heresies of your age; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble band.

08-31.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
08-31.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

08-31.title: Kontakion of the Placing of the Sash of the Theotokos
08-31.text: Like a robe of purple woven by faith, thy honoured Sash is placed in Constantinople, O Theotokos, as a defense and a strong wall unto the imperial city.

09-01.title: Troparion of St. Symeon the Stylite
09-01.text: Thou didst prove to be a pillar of patience and didst emulate the Forefathers, O righteous one: Job in his sufferings, Joseph in his temptations, the angelic life among them in the flesh. O Symeon, our righteous Father, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

09-01.title: Kontakion of the Indiction and St. Symeon the Stylite
09-01.text: Today the new year of the Church beginneth, and the Maker of all crowneth her with His blessings; and with the year we honour Symeon the Stylite, who upon a pillar did sanctify the air.

09-02.title: Troparion of Martyr Mamas of Caesarea
09-02.text: Thy Martyr Mamas of Caesarea, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-02.title: Kontakion of Martyr Mamas
09-02.text: From thy youth thou wast nurtured in the wilderness of Caesarea by the milk of a doe, O Mamas, and didst contend nobly for Christ; wherefore the Church doth honour thy memory.

09-03.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Anthimus
09-03.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Anthimus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-03.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Anthimus
09-03.text: Like a noble shepherd of Nicomedia, O Anthimus, thou didst stand before the tyrant Maximian, and didst pour forth thy blood as a libation for thy flock.

09-04.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Babylas of Antioch; Prophet Moses the God-Seer
09-04.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Babylas of Antioch; Prophet Moses the God-Seer, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-04.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Babylas and Prophet Moses
09-04.text: Confessing Christ before the Emperor Decius, O Babylas, thou didst forbid him entry into the church; and with thee we hymn the great Moses, the God-seer upon Sinai.

09-05.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Zechariah
09-05.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Zechariah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

09-05.title: Kontakion of Prophet Zechariah, father of the Forerunner
09-05.text: Slain by the impious within the temple itself, O Zechariah, thou didst seal thy priesthood with thy blood, and thy son the Forerunner did receive thy crown.

09-06.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
09-06.text: Captains of the heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy beseech thee that by your supplications thou wilt encompass us beneath the wings of thy immaterial glory, and faithfully preserve us who fall down and cry to thee: deliver us from all dangers, for ye are the commanders of the powers on high.

09-06.title: Kontakion of the Miracle of the Archangel Michael at Chonae
