09-06.text: Captain of the heavenly hosts, O Michael, thou didst deliver the church at Chonae by striking the rock and channeling the waters; wherefore we honour thy mighty defense.

09-07.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
09-07.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

09-07.title: Kontakion of the Forefeast of the Nativity of the Theotokos and Martyr Sozon
09-07.text: Today the gates of holiness are opening, and the Most Pure Virgin is about to be born; and with it we honour Sozon the martyr, who broke the golden idol of Cilicia.

09-08.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
09-08.text: Thy nativity, O Theotokos, has proclaimed joy to the whole universe: for from thee did shine forth the Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God. He hath loosed the curse and granted the blessing; He hath overcome death and bestowed on us eternal life.

09-08.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
09-08.text: By thy nativity, O most pure Virgin, Joachim and Anna were freed from the reproach of barrenness, and Adam and Eve from the corruption of death. Thy people, redeemed from the guilt of their sins, exult and cry to thee: 'The barren one bringeth forth the Theotokos and the nourisher of our Life.'

09-09.title: Troparion of the Ancestors of God Joachim and Anna
09-09.text: O Joachim and Anna, righteous ancestors of God, ye have received from God the freedom that ye sought through long childlessness — for from your loins was sprung the Virgin who bore the Lord. Pray with her, that He grant us great mercy.

09-09.title: Kontakion of Sts. Joachim and Anna
09-09.text: Once barren and ashamed before the people, ye were vouchsafed to bring forth the dwelling-place of God, O righteous Joachim and Anna; wherefore the Church doth honour you as ancestors of God.

09-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Menodora
09-10.text: Thy Martyrs Menodora, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-10.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora
09-10.text: Threefold band of virgin-martyrs of Bithynia, ye contended for Christ together, O Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble sisterhood.

09-11.title: Troparion of St. Theodora of Alexandria
09-11.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Theodora of Alexandria, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

09-11.title: Kontakion of St. Theodora of Alexandria
09-11.text: Concealing thy womanhood under the disguise of a man, O Theodora, thou didst dwell in a monastery of monks, and didst endure great trials in steadfast humility.

09-12.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Autonomus
09-12.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Autonomus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-12.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Autonomus
09-12.text: Once a bishop in Italy, thou didst flee unto Bithynia from the persecutors, O Autonomus, and there sealed thy hierarchy with the witness of martyrdom.

09-13.title: Troparion of Commemoration of the Dedication of the Church of the Resurrection at Jerusalem
09-13.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Commemoration of the Dedication of the Church of the Resurrection at Jerusalem, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

09-13.title: Kontakion of the Dedication of the Resurrection Church and the Forefeast of the Cross
09-13.text: Today the Church of the Resurrection is consecrated in Jerusalem, and tomorrow the Honoured Cross is exalted; wherefore the faithful rejoice with twofold joy.

09-14.title: Troparion of the Exaltation of the Cross
09-14.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance. Grant victory to the Orthodox Christians over their adversaries, and by the power of Thy Cross, preserve Thy commonwealth.

09-14.title: Kontakion of the Exaltation of the Cross
09-14.text: O Christ our God, Thou wast lifted up upon the Cross of Thine own will. Therefore grant Thy mercies unto Thy new commonwealth which is called by Thy name. By Thy power gladden the Orthodox Christians, and grant them victory over every adversary, having as an ally that weapon of peace, the trophy invincible.

09-15.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Nicetas the Goth
09-15.text: Thy Great-martyr Nicetas the Goth, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-15.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Nicetas the Goth
09-15.text: Once a Gothic prince, O Nicetas, thou didst convert thy people to Christ, and didst seal thy faith by fire from the hand of the Arian Athanaric.

09-16.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Euphemia the All-praised
09-16.text: Thy Great-martyr Euphemia the All-praised, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-16.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Euphemia
09-16.text: Once a maiden of Chalcedon, thou didst contend nobly, O Euphemia all-praised; and at the Fourth Council thy relics did receive the orthodox tome and rejected that of the heretics.

09-17.title: Troparion of Sophia and her Daughters Faith, Hope, and Love
09-17.text: The Church doth celebrate and praise as three sweet-scented flowers Faith, Hope and Love, divinely thriving; and crieth out in song to their mother Sophia, who is named for divine wisdom: Through the contests of these thy children entreat the only Lover of mankind that we be granted mercy and the divine vision.

09-17.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Sophia, Faith, Hope, and Love
09-17.text: Their mother bringeth them as a fragrant offering unto the Master, O Faith, Hope, and Love; and Sophia followeth her daughters thrice over by maternal sorrow and martyric joy.

09-18.title: Troparion of St. Eumenius the Wonderworker
09-18.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Eumenius the Wonderworker, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

09-18.title: Kontakion of St. Eumenius the Wonderworker
09-18.text: Bishop of Gortyna and worker of wonders, O Eumenius, thou didst tame a fierce dragon by prayer, and didst pour forth healings upon them that flee unto thee.

09-19.title: Troparion of Martyrs Trophimus
09-19.text: Thy Martyrs Trophimus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-19.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Trophimus, Sabbatius, and Dorymedon
09-19.text: Yoked together in confession before the tyrant Probus, ye contended unto the wreaths of martyrdom, O Trophimus, Sabbatius, and Dorymedon.

09-20.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Eustathius Placidas
09-20.text: Thy holy martyr Eustathius, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from Thee, our God. For having Thy strength, he laid low his adversaries, and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions, save our souls.

09-20.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Eustathius and his Family
09-20.text: Once Placidas, the noble general of Trajan, thou didst behold the Cross between the antlers of the stag, O Eustathius, and with thy wife and sons didst seal thy faith within a brazen bull.

09-21.title: Troparion of the Honorable Cross
09-21.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance, granting unto Thy people victory over all their enemies, and by the power of Thy Cross preserving Thy commonwealth.

09-21.title: Kontakion of the Leavetaking of the Cross and Apostle Quadratus
09-21.text: Today the feast of the Honoured Cross is sealed, and with it the apostle Quadratus is hymned, who at Athens did defend the Faith against the heathen by his Apologia.

09-22.title: Troparion of the Prophet Jonah
09-22.text: As thou didst foretell mystically the three-day burial of Christ, and didst preach repentance unto Nineveh, do thou ever pray for us, O Prophet Jonah.

09-22.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Phocas
09-22.text: Once a gardener of Sinope, thou didst dig thine own grave with thy hands, O Phocas, and didst lay down thy life for Christ; wherefore the seafarers cry unto thee for safe voyage.

09-23.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
09-23.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

09-23.title: Kontakion of the Conception of the Forerunner
09-23.text: Today the universe rejoiceth at the conception of the Forerunner of the Master, and Zechariah dance unto the silenced word; for he who shall point unto the Lamb of God is now formed in the womb.

09-24.title: Troparion of the Protomartyr Thekla
09-24.text: O Equal-to-the-Apostles first-suffering virgin Thekla, by the teachings of Paul thou didst put on the armor of Christ, and didst master the imaginings of evil; thou didst overthrow the tortures of the impious, and by the sign of the Cross thou didst turn aside the violence of nature. Pray for us, that He may grant our souls great mercy.

09-24.title: Kontakion of Protomartyr Thecla
09-24.text: Disciple of Paul and first among the female martyrs, O Thecla, thou didst confess Christ before the fire and the wild beasts, and didst escape unharmed by the providence of the Master.

09-25.title: Troparion of St. Sergius of Radonezh
09-25.text: Champion of virtues, true warrior of Christ our God: thou didst struggle greatly against the passions in this transient life, in psalmody, vigil, and fasting, becoming an example to thy disciples. Therefore the All-Holy Spirit dwelt in thee, and by His action thou wast adorned with splendor; and since thou hast great boldness before the Holy Trinity, remember the flock thou hast gathered, wise one, and as thou didst promise, do not forget to visit thy children, O venerable Sergius, our Father.

09-25.title: Kontakion of St. Euphrosyne and St. Sergius of Radonezh
09-25.text: Together we hymn Euphrosyne, who in the disguise of a man did dwell in a monastery of monks; and with her, holy Sergius of Radonezh, father of all the Russian land.

09-26.title: Troparion of Repose of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian
09-26.text: O Holy Apostle Repose of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

09-26.title: Kontakion of the Repose of John the Theologian
09-26.text: Who can recount thy mighty works, O Virgin Apostle? For thou pourest forth miracles and effusions of healings, and prayest for our souls as Theologian and Friend of Christ.

09-27.title: Troparion of Martyr Callistratus and the forty-nine soldiers with him
09-27.text: Thy Martyr Callistratus and the forty-nine soldiers with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-27.title: Kontakion of Martyr Callistratus and Companions
09-27.text: Like a chosen band of forty-nine soldiers, O Callistratus, ye contended together for Christ, and ye received together the wreaths of martyrdom.

09-28.title: Troparion of St. Chariton the Confessor
09-28.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Chariton the Confessor, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

09-28.title: Kontakion of St. Chariton the Confessor
09-28.text: Founder of the laura of the Old Cells, O Chariton, thou didst plant the desert of Judea with monastic flowers; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a venerable father.

09-29.title: Troparion of St. Cyriacus the Hermit
09-29.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Cyriacus the Hermit, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

09-29.title: Kontakion of St. Cyriacus the Hermit
09-29.text: Dwelling in the wilderness of Palestine for sixty and ten years, O Cyriacus, thou didst pour forth wonders by prayer, and didst confute the Origenist heretics by thy steadfast Faith.

09-30.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Gregory the Illuminator of Armenia
09-30.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Gregory the Illuminator of Armenia, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-30.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Gregory the Illuminator
09-30.text: Equal-to-the-Apostles, thou didst convert the kingdom of Armenia, O Gregory, and didst water it with the streams of preaching; wherefore the Church doth bless thee as enlightener.

10-01.title: Troparion of St. Romanos the Melodist
10-01.text: In thee, O Father, was carefully preserved what is according to the image; for, having taken up thy cross, thou didst follow Christ; by thy deeds thou hast taught us to despise the flesh, for it passeth away, but to attend to the soul as a thing immortal: wherefore, O venerable Romanos, thy spirit rejoiceth with the angels.

10-01.title: Kontakion of the Protection of the Theotokos
10-01.text: Today the Virgin standeth in the church and unseen prayeth God for us; the Angels worship with the Hierarchs, and the Apostles rejoice with the Prophets, for the Theotokos prayeth on our behalf to the Eternal God.

10-02.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Cyprian and Martyr Justina
10-02.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Cyprian and Martyr Justina, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-02.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Cyprian and Martyr Justina
10-02.text: Once a sorcerer of darkness, thou wast won by the steadfast purity of Justina, O Cyprian; and with her thou didst seal thy turning unto Christ by the witness of blood.

10-03.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Dionysius the Areopagite
10-03.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Dionysius the Areopagite, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-03.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Dionysius the Areopagite
10-03.text: Of the seventy disciples of the Master, thou wast called from the court of Areopagus by the preaching of Paul, O Dionysius, and didst seal thy hierarchy with martyrdom in Paris.

10-04.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Hierotheus of Athens
10-04.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Hierotheus of Athens, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-04.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Hierotheus
10-04.text: Of the seventy disciples and teacher of Dionysius the Areopagite, O Hierotheus, thou didst seal thy bishopric of Athens by the witness of blood.

10-05.title: Troparion of Martyr Charitina of Amisus
10-05.text: Thy Martyr Charitina of Amisus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-05.title: Kontakion of Martyr Charitina
10-05.text: Once a virgin of Amisus, thou didst stand before the tyrant unshaken, O Charitina, and didst seal thy confession by the witness of martyrdom.

10-06.title: Troparion of the Apostle Thomas
10-06.text: Holy Apostle Thomas, intercede with the merciful God that He grant unto our souls forgiveness of sins.

10-06.title: Kontakion of Apostle Thomas
10-06.text: Thomas the disciple of Christ, the wise initiate of God, with his right hand of faith probed Thy life-bestowing side, O Christ; and from thence he preached Thy great mercy unto India.

10-07.title: Troparion of Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus
10-07.text: Thy Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-07.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus
10-07.text: Yokefellows in arms and in martyrdom, ye were stripped of your military rank and clothed with the wreath of victory, O Sergius and Bacchus.

10-08.title: Troparion of St. Pelagia the Penitent
10-08.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Pelagia the Penitent, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

10-08.title: Kontakion of St. Pelagia the Penitent
10-08.text: Once a famed harlot of Antioch, thou wast won unto Christ by the tears of the bishop Nonnus, O Pelagia, and didst seal thy repentance by ascetic struggle in Jerusalem.

10-09.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle James
10-09.text: O Holy Apostle James, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-09.title: Kontakion of Apostle James, son of Alphaeus
10-09.text: Like a steadfast pillar of the Church, O James, son of Alphaeus, thou didst preach Christ unto the nations, and didst seal thy apostleship with the wreath of martyrdom.

10-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia
10-10.text: Thy Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-10.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia
10-10.text: Brother and sister joined in the wreath of martyrdom, O Eulampius and Eulampia, ye contended at Nicomedia, and unhurt by the fire didst find the heavenly bridechamber.

10-11.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Philip
10-11.text: O Holy Apostle Philip, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-11.title: Kontakion of Apostle Philip the Deacon
10-11.text: One of the seven first-deacons, O Philip, thou didst baptize the eunuch of Ethiopia, and didst preach Christ throughout Samaria; wherefore the Church doth honour thy memory.

10-12.title: Troparion of Martyrs Probus
10-12.text: Thy Martyrs Probus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-12.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Probus, Tarachus, and Andronicus
10-12.text: Threefold band of martyrs of Tarsus, ye stood before the tribunals unshaken, O Probus, Tarachus, and Andronicus; wherefore the Church doth crown your contest.

10-13.title: Troparion of Martyrs Carpus
10-13.text: Thy Martyrs Carpus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-13.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Carpus and Companions
10-13.text: Bishop, deacon, and laypeople yoked together in the wreath of martyrdom, O Carpus, Papylus, Agathonike, and Agathodorus, ye contended at Pergamon for Christ.

10-14.title: Troparion of Martyrs Nazarius
10-14.text: Thy Martyrs Nazarius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-14.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Nazarius and Companions
10-14.text: Like a fourfold band of confessors ye contended at Milan, O Nazarius, Gervasius, Protasius, and Celsus, and didst pour forth your blood as a libation for Christ.

10-15.title: Troparion of St. Euthymius the New of Thessalonica
10-15.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Euthymius the New of Thessalonica, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

10-15.title: Kontakion of St. Euthymius the New
10-15.text: Like the Great Euthymius of old, thou didst gather monastics on Mount Athos, O Euthymius the New, and didst pour forth a fountain of teaching unto thy flock.

10-16.title: Troparion of Martyr Longinus the Centurion
10-16.text: Thy Martyr Longinus the Centurion, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-16.title: Kontakion of Martyr Longinus the Centurion
10-16.text: Once the centurion who confessed Christ upon the Cross, O Longinus, thou didst preach Him afterwards in Cappadocia, and didst seal thy confession by martyrdom.

10-17.title: Troparion of Lazarus the Four-Days-Dead
10-17.text: Giving us before thy Passion an assurance of the general resurrection, thou didst raise Lazarus from the dead, O Christ our God. Wherefore, like the children of old, we also bear before thee the symbols of triumph and cry to thee, the Vanquisher of death: Hosanna in the highest; blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

10-17.title: Kontakion of Prophet Hosea
10-17.text: Faithful prophet of the Lord, O Hosea, thou didst rebuke Israel by thy own marriage, foreshadowing the union of Christ with His Church; wherefore the Church doth honour thy memory.

10-18.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist Luke
10-18.text: O holy Apostle and Evangelist Luke, intercede with the merciful God that He grant unto our souls forgiveness of sins.

10-18.title: Kontakion of Evangelist Luke
10-18.text: Disciple of Paul and faithful physician, thou didst write the third Gospel and the Acts of the Apostles, O Luke; and didst paint the first icon of the Theotokos with thine own hand.

10-19.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Joel
10-19.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Joel, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

10-19.title: Kontakion of Prophet Joel
10-19.text: Foreteller of the descent of the Spirit upon all flesh, O Joel, thou didst herald the Day of the Lord; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

10-20.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Artemius of Antioch
10-20.text: Thy Great-martyr Artemius of Antioch, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-20.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Artemius
10-20.text: Once a general of Antioch under Constantine and Constantius, thou didst rebuke the apostate Julian, O Artemius, and didst seal thy faith by the wreath of martyrdom.

10-21.title: Troparion of St. Hilarion the Great
10-21.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Hilarion the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

10-21.title: Kontakion of St. Hilarion the Great
10-21.text: Father of monastic life in Palestine and disciple of Anthony, O Hilarion, thou didst pour forth wonders by prayer, and didst plant the wilderness with the flowers of asceticism.

10-22.title: Troparion of Equal-to-the-Apostles Abercius
10-22.text: O Holy Apostle Equal-to-the-Apostles Abercius, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-22.title: Kontakion of Equal-to-the-Apostles Abercius
10-22.text: Wonderworker and bishop of Hierapolis in Phrygia, O Abercius, thou didst journey unto Rome to heal the emperor's daughter; wherefore the Church doth bless thy memory.

10-23.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle James, Brother of the Lord
10-23.text: As the Lord's disciple thou didst receive the Gospel, O righteous one; as a martyr thou hast that which cannot be despised; thou hast boldness as the Brother of God; as a hierarch thou hast the gift of intercession. Pray to Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-23.title: Kontakion of Apostle James, Brother of the Lord
10-23.text: First bishop of Jerusalem and brother of the Lord according to the flesh, O James the Just, thou didst write the Catholic Epistle and didst seal thy hierarchy with martyrdom.

10-24.title: Troparion of Martyr Arethas and his companions of Najran
10-24.text: Thy Martyr Arethas and his companions of Najran, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-24.title: Kontakion of Martyr Arethas and Companions
10-24.text: Once an elder of the city of Najran, thou didst lead a great band of Christians unto the wreath of martyrdom, O Arethas, refusing the demands of the apostate Dhu Nuwas.

10-25.title: Troparion of Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius the Notaries
10-25.text: Thy Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius the Notaries, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-25.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius
10-25.text: Notaries of Paul the Confessor, ye contended for the truth of Orthodoxy, O Marcian and Martyrius, and didst seal the Faith with the witness of your blood.

10-26.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Demetrius of Thessalonica
10-26.text: The world has found in thee a great champion in time of trouble, as thou didst defeat the heathen, O victorious one. As thou didst stop the boasting of Lyaeus, and inspire Nestor to courage in combat, so, O holy great-martyr Demetrius, entreat Christ God to grant us great mercy.

10-26.title: Kontakion of St. Demetrius
10-26.text: God, who gave thee invincible strength and with care kept thy city unharmed, royally clothed the Church in the purple of thy blood, for thou art her strength, O Demetrius.

10-27.title: Troparion of Martyr Nestor of Thessalonica
10-27.text: Thy Martyr Nestor of Thessalonica, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-27.title: Kontakion of Martyr Nestor of Thessalonica
10-27.text: Disciple of the great Demetrius, thou wast blessed by him for the contest, O Nestor, and didst slay the giant Lyaeus before the eyes of Maximian; wherefore the Church honoureth thee.

10-28.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Paraskeva
10-28.text: O Paraskeva, athlete worthy of all praise, thou didst contend in suffering, and the Bridegroom Christ adorned thee with the crown of incorruption. Therefore intercede with Him that our souls may be saved.

10-28.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Terence, Neonilla, and their Children
10-28.text: Like a noble household of martyrs, O Terence and Neonilla, with your seven children ye contended for Christ, and ye received together the wreaths of victory.

10-29.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Anastasia the Roman
10-29.text: Thy lamb Anastasia the Roman, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

10-29.title: Kontakion of Virgin-martyr Anastasia the Roman
10-29.text: Once a young maiden of Rome, thou didst forsake the world for the convent, O Anastasia, and at thy mistress's hand didst find the wreath of martyric purity.

10-30.title: Troparion of Martyr Zenobius and his sister Zenobia
10-30.text: Thy Martyr Zenobius and his sister Zenobia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-30.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Zenobius and Zenobia
10-30.text: Brother and sister of one flesh and of one wreath of martyrdom, O Zenobius and Zenobia, ye gave alms unto the poor, and so were vouchsafed to follow Christ unto martyrdom.

10-31.title: Troparion of Apostles Stachys
10-31.text: O Holy Apostles Stachys, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-31.title: Kontakion of Apostles Stachys and Companions
10-31.text: As a sixfold band of the seventy disciples, ye preached Christ in many lands, O Stachys, Amplias, Urban, Narcissus, Apelles, and Aristobulus; wherefore the Church doth hymn your memory.

11-01.title: Troparion of Sts. Cosmas and Damian, Unmercenaries of Asia
11-01.text: O holy Unmercenaries and wonderworkers Cosmas and Damian, visit our infirmities; freely you have received, freely give to us.

11-01.title: Kontakion of Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Asia
11-01.text: Having received the grace of healings, ye bestow healing without payment upon them that flee unto you, O glorious Cosmas and Damian of Asia; and pourest forth streams of mercy upon all the faithful.

11-02.title: Troparion of Martyrs Acindynus
11-02.text: Thy Martyrs Acindynus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-02.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Acindynus and Companions
11-02.text: As a fivefold band of confessors of Persia, ye contended together for Christ, O Acindynus, Pegasius, Aphthonius, Elpidiphorus, and Anempodistus; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble contest.

11-03.title: Troparion of Martyrs Acepsimas the Bishop
11-03.text: Thy Martyrs Acepsimas the Bishop, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-03.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Acepsimas, Joseph, and Aeithalas
11-03.text: Threefold band of clergy of Persia — bishop, presbyter, and deacon — ye sealed your hierarchy with the wreath of martyrdom, O Acepsimas, Joseph, and Aeithalas.

11-04.title: Troparion of St. Joannicius the Great of Bithynia
11-04.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Joannicius the Great of Bithynia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

11-04.title: Kontakion of St. Joannicius the Great
11-04.text: Once a soldier of the imperial guard, thou didst forsake the world for the wilderness of Bithynia, O Joannicius, and didst pour forth wonders upon them that flee unto thee.

11-05.title: Troparion of Martyrs Galaction and Episteme
11-05.text: Thy Martyrs Galaction and Episteme, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-05.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Galaction and Episteme
11-05.text: Once a noble pair of Christian wedlock, ye chose virginity for the love of Christ, O Galaction and Episteme, and didst seal thy purity by the wreath of martyrdom.

11-06.title: Troparion of St. Paul the Confessor
11-06.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Paul the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

11-06.title: Kontakion of St. Paul the Confessor
11-06.text: Patriarch of the New Rome and steadfast confessor of the Faith of Nicaea, O Paul, thou didst endure exile from the Arians, and didst seal thy hierarchy by martyrdom in Cappadocia.

11-07.title: Troparion of Martyr Hieron of Melitene and thirty-three companions
11-07.text: Thy Martyr Hieron of Melitene and thirty-three companions, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-07.title: Kontakion of Martyr Hieron and Companions
11-07.text: Leading thirty-three companions unto the wreath of martyrdom, O Hieron, thou didst seal thy faith by the witness of blood at Melitene.

11-08.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
11-08.text: O Captains of the heavenly armies, we who are unworthy ever beseech you to deliver us by your supplications from terrors and to cover us with the protection of the wings of your immaterial glory, even as we fall down and cry to you: deliver us from danger, O captains of the angelic hosts.

11-08.title: Kontakion of the Synaxis of the Archangel Michael
11-08.text: Captains supreme of God's heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy ever entreat you, that by your prayers ye encompass us beneath the wings of your unseen glory; safeguard us as we fervently fall down and cry: From dangers deliver us, since ye are the marshals of the powers on high.

11-09.title: Troparion of Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius; St. Matrona of Constantinople
11-09.text: Thy Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius; St. Matrona of Constantinople, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-09.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius
11-09.text: Joined together by the bond of love, O Onesiphorus and Porphyrius, ye contended unto the wreaths of martyrdom; with you we hymn Matrona, mother of monastics of Constantinople.

11-10.title: Troparion of Apostles Erastus
11-10.text: O Holy Apostles Erastus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

11-10.title: Kontakion of Apostles Erastus and Companions
11-10.text: As a sixfold band of apostles of the seventy, ye preached Christ unto many lands, O Erastus, Olympas, Rodion, Sosipater, Quartus, and Tertius.

11-11.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Menas of Egypt
11-11.text: Thy Great-martyr Menas of Egypt, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-11.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Menas
11-11.text: Once a soldier of Cotyaeum in Phrygia, thou didst forsake thy rank for the love of Christ, O Menas, and within the desert of Egypt didst seal thy faith with martyrdom.

11-12.title: Troparion of St. John the Merciful
11-12.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father John the Merciful, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

11-12.title: Kontakion of St. John the Merciful and St. Nilus the Faster
11-12.text: Patriarch of Alexandria and dispenser of mercy unto the poor, O John, with thee we hymn Nilus the Faster of Sinai, who wrote the desert sayings of holiness.

11-13.title: Troparion of St. John Chrysostom
11-13.text: Grace shining forth from thy lips like a beacon hath enlightened the universe, and disclosed to the world the riches of poverty; it hath shown us the heights of humility. As thou dost instruct us by thy words, O John Chrysostom, our Father, so intercede with the Word, Christ our God, to save our souls.

11-13.title: Kontakion of St. John Chrysostom
11-13.text: Thou didst receive divine grace from heaven, and with thy lips thou didst teach all to worship the One God in Trinity, O all-blessed and venerable Chrysostom; rightly we acclaim thee, for thou art our master, revealing things divine.

11-14.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Philip
11-14.text: Holy Apostle Philip, intercede with the merciful God that He grant unto our souls forgiveness of sins.

11-14.title: Kontakion of Apostle Philip
11-14.text: Disciple and friend of Christ and imitator of His Passion, O Philip, thou didst preach Him unto the heathen, and didst seal thy apostleship with the upside-down cross in Hierapolis.

11-15.title: Troparion of Beginning of the Nativity Fast; Martyrs and Confessors Gurias
11-15.text: Thy Martyr Beginning of the Nativity Fast; Martyrs and Confessors Gurias, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-15.title: Kontakion of the Beginning of the Nativity Fast and Martyrs Gurias, Samonas, and Habib
11-15.text: Today the Church beginneth the fast that prepareth for the coming of the Word in the flesh; and with it we honour Gurias, Samonas, and Habib, defenders of Christian wedlock.

11-16.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist Matthew
11-16.text: With zealous following thou didst hearken to Christ the Master who called thee, and having spurned the income of the custom-house thou didst show forth as a most-praised apostle and evangelist. Therefore, all-praised Matthew, entreat Christ God to grant remission of sins to those who honor thy memory.

11-16.title: Kontakion of Apostle Matthew
11-16.text: Once a publican at the seat of customs, thou didst forsake all to follow Christ, O Matthew, and didst write the first Gospel for the Hebrews; wherefore the Church doth hymn thy memory.

11-17.title: Troparion of Gregory the Wonderworker
11-17.text: Thou didst become worthy of thy name through thy manner of life; through thy faith, O wonderworker Gregory, the city of Neocaesarea is well established. Wherefore, having boldness before Christ God, entreat Him to save our souls.

11-17.title: Kontakion of St. Gregory the Wonderworker
11-17.text: Wonderworker and bishop of Neo-Caesarea, O Gregory, thou didst command the mountains by prayer, and didst leave but seventeen pagans in thy city; wherefore the Church doth hymn thee.

11-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Plato of Ancyra
11-18.text: Thy Martyr Plato of Ancyra, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-18.title: Kontakion of Martyr Plato of Ancyra
11-18.text: Once a noble youth of Ancyra, thou didst forsake the riches of this world, O Plato, and didst seal thy confession of Christ by the wreath of martyrdom.

11-19.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Obadiah
11-19.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Obadiah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

11-19.title: Kontakion of Prophet Obadiah
11-19.text: Faithful prophet of the Lord, O Obadiah, thou didst foretell the judgment of Edom and the salvation of Sion; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

11-20.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
11-20.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

11-20.title: Kontakion of the Forefeast of the Entrance and St. Gregory the Decapolite
11-20.text: Today the gates of holiness are opening, for the most pure Temple shall enter the Temple; and with this forefeast we honour Gregory of the Decapolis, ascetic of Olympus.

11-21.title: Troparion of the Entrance of the Theotokos
11-21.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of mankind's salvation. The Virgin appeareth openly in the temple of God, and foretelleth Christ to all. Unto her, then, let us also cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's counsel.

11-21.title: Kontakion of the Entrance of the Theotokos
11-21.text: The most pure temple of the Saviour, the very precious bridal chamber and Virgin, the holy treasure of the glory of God, is led on this day into the house of the Lord, bringing with her the grace of the Divine Spirit. Of her doth the angels of God sing: 'Behold the heavenly tabernacle.'

11-22.title: Troparion of Apostles Philemon and Archippus
11-22.text: O Holy Apostles Philemon and Archippus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

11-22.title: Kontakion of Apostles Philemon, Archippus, and Apphia
11-22.text: Threefold household of the Faith hymned by Paul, ye sealed your apostleship with the wreath of martyrdom, O Philemon, Archippus, and Apphia.

11-23.title: Troparion of Sts. Amphilochius of Iconium and Gregory of Acragas
11-23.text: O holy Sts. Amphilochius of Iconium and Gregory of Acragas, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

11-23.title: Kontakion of Sts. Amphilochius and Gregory
11-23.text: Companion of the Cappadocians, O Amphilochius of Iconium, thou didst confound the heresies; and with thee we hymn Gregory of Acragas, faithful bishop of Sicily.

11-24.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Catherine
11-24.text: Let us praise the most-honorable bride of Christ, the divine Catherine, protectress of Sinai; for she silenced the eloquence of the impious by the power of the Spirit, and being crowned as a martyr, she now asks for all great mercy.

11-24.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Catherine
11-24.text: Once a noble maiden of Alexandria most wise in philosophy, thou didst confound fifty orators of the emperor Maxentius, O Catherine, and upon the wheel didst seal thy confession.

11-25.title: Troparion of Leavetaking of the Entrance; Hieromartyr Clement of Rome
11-25.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Leavetaking of the Entrance; Hieromartyr Clement of Rome, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

11-25.title: Kontakion of the Leavetaking of the Entrance and Hieromartyr Clement
11-25.text: Today the feast of the Entrance is sealed, and with it we honour Clement, third bishop of Rome, disciple of Peter and Paul, who within the depths of the sea did receive a temple of stone.

11-26.title: Troparion of St. Alypius the Stylite of Adrianopolis
11-26.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Alypius the Stylite of Adrianopolis, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

11-26.title: Kontakion of St. Alypius the Stylite
11-26.text: Standing for fifty and three years upon a pillar at Adrianopolis, O Alypius, thou didst gather a chorus of disciples beneath thee; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a wonder of patience.

11-27.title: Troparion of Great-martyr James the Persian
11-27.text: Thy Great-martyr James the Persian, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-27.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr James the Persian
11-27.text: Cut piece by piece for the Faith of Christ, O James the Persian, thou didst bless each limb taken from thee, and didst find the heavenly bridechamber unmaimed.

11-28.title: Troparion of Martyr Stephen the New of Mt. St. Auxentius
11-28.text: Thy Martyr Stephen the New of Mt. St. Auxentius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-28.title: Kontakion of Martyr Stephen the New
11-28.text: Confessing the icons of Christ upon Mount St. Auxentius, O Stephen the New, thou didst stand alone against the iconoclast Copronymus, and didst seal thy confession beneath the stones of the rabble.

11-29.title: Troparion of Martyr Paramonus and three hundred and seventy with him
11-29.text: Thy Martyr Paramonus and three hundred and seventy with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-29.title: Kontakion of Martyr Paramonus and Companions
11-29.text: Leading three hundred and seventy other martyrs unto the wreath of victory, O Paramonus, thou didst stand before the tyrant Aquilinus unshaken; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble band.

11-30.title: Troparion of the First-Called Apostle Andrew
11-30.text: O Apostle Andrew, first-called of all the Twelve and brother of him who was foremost of the chief Apostles, beseech the Master of all to grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

11-30.title: Kontakion of Apostle Andrew the First-Called
11-30.text: Let us praise the namesake of courage, the divinely-eloquent and first-called of the disciples of the Saviour, the brother of Peter; for as he called of old to him, so now to us he crieth: Come, we have found the One whom the world desireth.

12-01.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Nahum
12-01.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Nahum, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-01.title: Kontakion of Prophet Nahum
12-01.text: Like a fearful seer of the wrath of God, O Nahum, thou didst foretell the fall of Nineveh; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

12-02.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Habakkuk
12-02.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Habakkuk, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-02.title: Kontakion of Prophet Habakkuk
12-02.text: Standing upon thy watchtower of prayer, O Habakkuk, thou didst hear from the Lord the answer of His promise; wherefore the Church doth honour thee as a great prophet.

12-03.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Zephaniah
12-03.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Zephaniah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-03.title: Kontakion of Prophet Zephaniah
12-03.text: Filled with the grace of prophecy, O Zephaniah, thou didst foretell the great Day of the Lord; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

12-04.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Barbara
12-04.text: Let us honor the holy Barbara, for as a bird she escaped the snares of the enemy, and destroyed them through the help and defense of the Cross.

12-04.title: Troparion of St. John of Damascus
12-04.text: Orthodox image of the truth, you proved yourself to be a model of monastic life as well as of confession; as a true source of wisdom you sang inspired hymns. O wise and venerable John, pray to Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

12-04.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Barbara
12-04.text: Singing the praises of the Trinity in the heart of three windows, O Barbara, thou didst confound the unbelief of thy father, and didst seal thy faith with the witness of thine own blood.

12-05.title: Troparion of St. Sabbas the Sanctified
12-05.text: Thou didst prove to be a citizen of the desert, an angel in the flesh, and a wonderworker, O Sabbas, our God-bearing father. By fasting, vigil, and prayer thou didst obtain heavenly gifts, and thou healest the sick and the souls of those who turn to thee in faith. Glory to Him who gave thee strength; glory to Him who crowned thee; glory to Him who worketh healings for all through thee.

12-05.title: Kontakion of St. Sabbas the Sanctified
12-05.text: As a noble plant of asceticism, thou didst water the wilderness of Judea with thy tears, O Sabbas the Sanctified, and didst found there the Great Lavra of monastic life.

12-06.title: Troparion of St. Nicholas
12-06.text: The truth of things hath revealed thee to thy flock as a rule of faith, an icon of meekness, and a teacher of temperance; for which cause thou hast achieved the heights by humility, riches by poverty. O Father and Hierarch Nicholas, intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-06.title: Kontakion of St. Nicholas
12-06.text: Thou wast a true minister at Myra, O Saint Nicholas, and didst fulfill Christ's Gospel, laying down thy life for thy people and saving the innocent from death. Therefore thou hast been sanctified, as a great minister of the grace of God.

12-07.title: Troparion of St. Ambrose
12-07.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Ambrose, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

12-07.title: Kontakion of St. Ambrose of Milan
12-07.text: Champion of Orthodoxy and bishop of Milan, O Ambrose, thou didst stand before emperors unshaken, and didst draw Augustine unto the streams of holy baptism.

12-08.title: Troparion of St. Patapius of Thebes
12-08.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Patapius of Thebes, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-08.title: Kontakion of St. Patapius of Thebes
12-08.text: Wonderworker and ascetic of Thebes, O Patapius, thou didst flee the world for the wilderness, and didst pour forth healings upon them that flee unto thy honoured relics.

12-09.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
12-09.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

12-09.title: Kontakion of the Conception of the Theotokos by Righteous Anna
12-09.text: Today the universe rejoiceth, for Anna conceiveth the Mother of God; for she who shall bring forth the ineffable Word is herself conceived as the harbinger of our salvation.

12-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Menas
12-10.text: Thy Martyrs Menas, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-10.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Menas, Hermogenes, and Eugraphus
12-10.text: Sent to subdue the rebellion, O Menas, thou didst become the leader of confessors instead; and with Hermogenes and Eugraphus ye sealed the Faith by the wreath of martyrdom.

12-11.title: Troparion of St. Daniel the Stylite
12-11.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Daniel the Stylite, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-11.title: Kontakion of St. Daniel the Stylite
12-11.text: Standing for three and thirty years upon a pillar, O Daniel, thou didst gather a chorus of disciples beneath thee, and didst pour forth wonders by prayer; wherefore the Church doth bless thee.

12-12.title: Troparion of St. Spyridon the Wonderworker
12-12.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Spyridon the Wonderworker, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

12-12.title: Kontakion of St. Spyridon of Trimythous
12-12.text: Wounded by Christ's love and graced with the wings of the Spirit, thou didst take wing unto Christ, O Spyridon, and didst confound the philosopher at Nicaea by the brick of the Trinity.

12-13.title: Troparion of St. Herman of Alaska
12-13.text: Joyful North Star of the Church of Christ, guiding all people to the Heavenly Kingdom; teacher and apostle of the True Faith; intercessor and defender of the oppressed; adornment of the Orthodox Church in America: Blessed Father Herman of Alaska, pray to our Lord Jesus Christ to save our souls.

12-13.title: Kontakion of the Five Companion Martyrs
12-13.text: Like a fivefold band of soldiers of Christ, ye contended at Sebaste, O Eustratius, Auxentius, Eugene, Mardarius, and Orestes, and ye received together the wreaths of martyrdom.

12-14.title: Troparion of Martyrs Thyrsus
12-14.text: Thy Martyrs Thyrsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-14.title: Kontakion of Martyrs Thyrsus and Companions
12-14.text: Like a fivefold band of confessors of Christ, ye contended at Apollonia, O Thyrsus, Leucius, Philemon, Apollonius, and Callinicus; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble contest.

12-15.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Eleutherius
12-15.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Eleutherius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-15.title: Kontakion of Hieromartyr Eleutherius
12-15.text: Once a youthful bishop of Illyria, O Eleutherius, thou didst stand before the tyrant Hadrian unshaken, and didst seal thy hierarchy with the wreath of martyrdom.

12-16.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Haggai
12-16.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Haggai, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-16.title: Kontakion of Prophet Haggai
12-16.text: Filled with the grace of prophecy, O Haggai, thou didst urge on the rebuilding of the temple after the captivity; wherefore the Church doth bless thy honoured memory.

12-17.title: Troparion of the Prophet Daniel and the Three Youths
12-17.text: Great are the achievements of faith! In the fountain of flame, as on the waters of rest, the three holy youths rejoiced. And the prophet Daniel was shown to be a shepherd of lions, as of sheep. Through their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

12-17.title: Kontakion of Prophet Daniel and the Three Holy Youths
12-17.text: Daniel was sealed within the lions' den and saved by an angel, and the three youths in the furnace did walk as in a meadow; wherefore the Church doth honour them as victors over fire and beasts.

12-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Sebastian and his companions at Rome
12-18.text: Thy Martyr Sebastian and his companions at Rome, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-18.title: Kontakion of Martyr Sebastian and Companions
12-18.text: Once a commander of the Roman guard, thou didst secretly nourish the faithful in prison, O Sebastian, and at last didst seal thy confession by the showers of arrows.

12-19.title: Troparion of Martyr Boniface of Tarsus
12-19.text: Thy Martyr Boniface of Tarsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-19.title: Kontakion of Martyr Boniface
12-19.text: Sent from Rome unto Tarsus to bring back the relics of martyrs, thou didst find thyself a martyr instead, O Boniface, and didst return as relic to thy mistress Aglae.

12-20.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Ignatius
12-20.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood, O Hieromartyr Ignatius. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-20.title: Kontakion of the Hieromartyr Ignatius
12-20.text: From the East today hath the radiant star arisen, illumining the faithful with the splendor of his miracles. Wherefore let us crown with hymns Ignatius the God-bearer, the heavenly worshipper.

12-21.title: Troparion of St. Juliana of Nicomedia
12-21.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Juliana of Nicomedia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-21.title: Kontakion of St. Juliana of Nicomedia
12-21.text: Once a noble maiden of Nicomedia, thou didst forsake an unfaithful suitor for Christ, O Juliana, and within prison didst confound the deceiver and seal thy faith with martyrdom.

12-22.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Anastasia
12-22.text: Thy lamb Anastasia cries out to Thee, O Jesus, with a great voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I run to Thee. I am crucified with Thee and baptized into Thy death; I suffer for Thy sake that I may reign with Thee, and die for Thee that I may live in Thee. As a spotless sacrifice receive her who with love is sacrificed for Thee. By her prayers, as Thou art merciful, save our souls.

12-22.title: Kontakion of Great-martyr Anastasia
12-22.text: Once a noble lady of Rome, thou didst visit the prisons and loose the bonds of the confessors, O Anastasia, and didst seal thy ministry by the wreath of martyrdom.

12-23.title: Troparion of Ten Martyrs of Crete
12-23.text: Thy Martyr Ten Martyrs of Crete, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-23.title: Kontakion of the Ten Martyrs of Crete
12-23.text: Like a tenfold chorus of confessors of Christ, ye contended at Gortyna under Decius, O ye ten martyrs of Crete; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble company.

12-24.title: Troparion of Eve of the Nativity of Christ; Virgin-martyr Eugenia of Rome
12-24.text: Thy lamb Eve of the Nativity of Christ; Virgin-martyr Eugenia of Rome, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

12-24.title: Kontakion of the Eve of the Nativity
12-24.text: Today the Virgin cometh unto the cave, ineffably to bring forth the Word eternal; hearing this rejoice, O universe, and with the Angels and shepherds glorify Him who shall appear, the Eternal God as a young Child.

12-25.title: Troparion of the Nativity of Christ
12-25.text: Thy Nativity, O Christ our God, hath shone forth upon the world the light of knowledge; for thereby they that worshipped the stars were taught by a star to worship Thee, the Sun of Righteousness, and to know Thee, the Dayspring from on high. O Lord, glory to Thee.

12-25.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of Christ
12-25.text: Today the Virgin giveth birth to Him who is transcendent in essence, and the earth offereth a cave to Him who is unapproachable. Angels with shepherds give glory, and Magi journey with a star, for to us is born a young Child, the pre-eternal God.

12-26.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
12-26.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

12-26.title: Kontakion of the Synaxis of the Theotokos
12-26.text: He who was begotten of the Father before the morning star, before the ages, hath been incarnate of thee today, O pure Maiden, and hath appeared upon earth; and we, glorifying His incarnation, do honour and bless thee, O sole Mother of God.

12-27.title: Troparion of the Protomartyr Stephen
12-27.text: O Protomartyr and servant of Christ, thou didst conquer in struggle and didst overthrow the wickedness of the enemies. As the first to be crowned, glorious Stephen, thou prayest now to Christ for the salvation of our souls.

12-27.title: Kontakion of Protomartyr Stephen
12-27.text: Yesterday the Master came unto us in the flesh, but today the servant departeth out of the flesh; yesterday He who is King in nature reigned, and today His witness is stoned for Him, even the Protomartyr Stephen.

12-28.title: Troparion of The 20
12-28.text: Thy Martyr The 20, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-28.title: Kontakion of the 20,000 Martyrs of Nicomedia
12-28.text: Twenty thousand strong ye stood for Christ within the church of Nicomedia, and unto the flames ye gave yourselves rejoicing; wherefore the Church doth crown your noble company.

12-29.title: Troparion of The 14
12-29.text: O holy The 14, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

12-29.title: Kontakion of the 14,000 Holy Innocents
12-29.text: When the Lord at His coming sojourned in Bethlehem, the unrighteous Herod sent his soldiers with cruel intent, that they might slay the infants; but the children, fashioned anew in the bath of immortality, did rejoice within Paradise.

12-30.title: Troparion of Martyr Anysia of Thessalonica
12-30.text: Thy Martyr Anysia of Thessalonica, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-30.title: Kontakion of Martyr Anysia of Thessalonica
12-30.text: Once a virgin of Thessalonica, thou wast slain by a Roman soldier as thou didst hasten unto the synaxis of the faithful, O Anysia; wherefore the Church doth crown thee with hymns.

12-31.title: Troparion of St. Melania the Younger of Rome
12-31.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Melania the Younger of Rome, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-31.title: Kontakion of St. Melania the Younger
12-31.text: Once a noble lady of Rome, thou didst give away thy wealth unto the poor, O Melania, and didst end thy days in monastic struggle in Jerusalem; wherefore the Church blesseth thee.
